2141 // X + Y overflows ? (Y >s 0 ? INTMAX : INTMIN) : X + Y --> sadd_sat X, Y
2142 // X + Y overflows ? (Y >s -1 ? INTMAX : INTMIN) : X + Y --> sadd_sat X, Y
2143 if (Pred == ICmpInst::ICMP_SGT && IsZeroOrOne(*C + 1) &&
2144 IsMinMax(FalseVal, TrueVal))
2145 return true;
2146 } else {
2147 // X - Y overflows ? (X <s 0 ? INTMIN : INTMAX) : X - Y --> ssub_sat X, Y
2148 // X - Y overflows ? (X <s -1 ? INTMIN : INTMAX) : X - Y --> ssub_sat X, Y
2149 if (Op == X && Pred == ICmpInst::ICMP_SLT && IsZeroOrOne(*C + 1) &&
2150 IsMinMax(TrueVal, FalseVal))
2151 return true;
2152 // X - Y overflows ? (X >s -1 ? INTMAX : INTMIN) : X - Y --> ssub_sat X, Y
2153 // X - Y overflows ? (X >s -2 ? INTMAX : INTMIN) : X - Y --> ssub_sat X, Y
2154 if (Op == X && Pred == ICmpInst::ICMP_SGT && IsZeroOrOne(*C + 2) &&
2155 IsMinMax(FalseVal, TrueVal))
2156 return true;
2157 // X - Y overflows ? (Y <s 0 ? INTMAX : INTMIN) : X - Y --> ssub_sat X, Y
2158 // X - Y overflows ? (Y <s 1 ? INTMAX : INTMIN) : X - Y --> ssub_sat X, Y
2159 if (Op == Y && Pred == ICmpInst::ICMP_SLT && IsZeroOrOne(*C) &&
2160 IsMinMax(FalseVal, TrueVal))
2161 return true;
2162 // X - Y overflows ? (Y >s 0 ? INTMIN : INTMAX) : X - Y --> ssub_sat X, Y
2163 // X - Y overflows ? (Y >s -1 ? INTMIN : INTMAX) : X - Y --> ssub_sat X, Y
2164 if (Op == Y && Pred == ICmpInst::ICMP_SGT && IsZeroOrOne(*C + 1) &&
2165 IsMinMax(TrueVal, FalseVal))
2166 return true;
2167 }
2168
2169 return false;
2170 };
2171
2172 Intrinsic::ID NewIntrinsicID;
2173 if (II->getIntrinsicID() == Intrinsic::uadd_with_overflow &&
2174 match(TrueVal, m_AllOnes()))
2175 // X + Y overflows ? -1 : X + Y -> uadd_sat X, Y
2176 NewIntrinsicID = Intrinsic::uadd_sat;
2177 else if (II->getIntrinsicID() == Intrinsic::usub_with_overflow &&
2178 match(TrueVal, m_Zero()))
2179 // X - Y overflows ? 0 : X - Y -> usub_sat X, Y
2180 NewIntrinsicID = Intrinsic::usub_sat;
2181 else if (II->getIntrinsicID() == Intrinsic::sadd_with_overflow &&
2182 IsSignedSaturateLimit(TrueVal, /*IsAdd=*/true))
2183 // X + Y overflows ? (X <s 0 ? INTMIN : INTMAX) : X + Y --> sadd_sat X, Y
2184 // X + Y overflows ? (X <s 1 ? INTMIN : INTMAX) : X + Y --> sadd_sat X, Y
2185 // X + Y overflows ? (X >s 0 ? INTMAX : INTMIN) : X + Y --> sadd_sat X, Y
2186 // X + Y overflows ? (X >s -1 ? INTMAX : INTMIN) : X + Y --> sadd_sat X, Y
2187 // X + Y overflows ? (Y <s 0 ? INTMIN : INTMAX) : X + Y --> sadd_sat X, Y
2188 // X + Y overflows ? (Y <s 1 ? INTMIN : INTMAX) : X + Y --> sadd_sat X, Y
2189 // X + Y overflows ? (Y >s 0 ? INTMAX : INTMIN) : X + Y --> sadd_sat X, Y
2190 // X + Y overflows ? (Y >s -1 ? INTMAX : INTMIN) : X + Y --> sadd_sat X, Y
2191 NewIntrinsicID = Intrinsic::sadd_sat;
2192 else if (II->getIntrinsicID() == Intrinsic::ssub_with_overflow &&
2193 IsSignedSaturateLimit(TrueVal, /*IsAdd=*/false))
2194 // X - Y overflows ? (X <s 0 ? INTMIN : INTMAX) : X - Y --> ssub_sat X, Y
2195 // X - Y overflows ? (X <s -1 ? INTMIN : INTMAX) : X - Y --> ssub_sat X, Y
2196 // X - Y overflows ? (X >s -1 ? INTMAX : INTMIN) : X - Y --> ssub_sat X, Y
2197 // X - Y overflows ? (X >s -2 ? INTMAX : INTMIN) : X - Y --> ssub_sat X, Y
2198 // X - Y overflows ? (Y <s 0 ? INTMAX : INTMIN) : X - Y --> ssub_sat X, Y
2199 // X - Y overflows ? (Y <s 1 ? INTMAX : INTMIN) : X - Y --> ssub_sat X, Y
2200 // X - Y overflows ? (Y >s 0 ? INTMIN : INTMAX) : X - Y --> ssub_sat X, Y
2201 // X - Y overflows ? (Y >s -1 ? INTMIN : INTMAX) : X - Y --> ssub_sat X, Y
2202 NewIntrinsicID = Intrinsic::ssub_sat;
2203 else
2204 return nullptr;
2205
2206 Function *F = Intrinsic::getOrInsertDeclaration(SI.getModule(),
2207 NewIntrinsicID, SI.getType());
2208 return CallInst::Create(F, {X, Y});
2209}
2210
2211Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::foldSelectExtConst(SelectInst &Sel) {
2212 Constant *C;
2213 if (!match(Sel.getTrueValue(), m_Constant(C)) &&
2214 !match(Sel.getFalseValue(), m_Constant(C)))
2215 return nullptr;
2216
2217 Instruction *ExtInst;
2218 if (!match(Sel.getTrueValue(), m_Instruction(ExtInst)) &&
2219 !match(Sel.getFalseValue(), m_Instruction(ExtInst)))
2220 return nullptr;
2221
2222 auto ExtOpcode = ExtInst->getOpcode();
2223 if (ExtOpcode != Instruction::ZExt && ExtOpcode != Instruction::SExt)
2224 return nullptr;
2225
2226 // If we are extending from a boolean type or if we can create a select that
2227 // has the same size operands as its condition, try to narrow the select.
2228 Value *X = ExtInst->getOperand(0);
2229 Type *SmallType = X->getType();
2230 Value *Cond = Sel.getCondition();
2231 auto *Cmp = dyn_cast<CmpInst>(Cond);
2232 if (!SmallType->isIntOrIntVectorTy(1) &&
2233 (!Cmp || Cmp->getOperand(0)->getType() != SmallType))
2234 return nullptr;
2235
2236 // If the constant is the same after truncation to the smaller type and
2237 // extension to the original type, we can narrow the select.
2238 Type *SelType = Sel.getType();
2239 Constant *TruncC = getLosslessTrunc(C, SmallType, ExtOpcode);
2240 if (TruncC && ExtInst->hasOneUse()) {
2241 Value *TruncCVal = cast<Value>(TruncC);
2242 if (ExtInst == Sel.getFalseValue())
2243 std::swap(X, TruncCVal);
2244
2245 // select Cond, (ext X), C --> ext(select Cond, X, C')
2246 // select Cond, C, (ext X) --> ext(select Cond, C', X)
2247 Value *NewSel = Builder.CreateSelect(Cond, X, TruncCVal, "narrow", &Sel);
2248 return CastInst::Create(Instruction::CastOps(ExtOpcode), NewSel, SelType);
2249 }
2250
2251 return nullptr;
2252}
2253
2254/// Try to transform a vector select with a constant condition vector into a
2255/// shuffle for easier combining with other shuffles and insert/extract.
2256static Instruction *canonicalizeSelectToShuffle(SelectInst &SI) {
2257 Value *CondVal = SI.getCondition();
2258 Constant *CondC;
2259 auto *CondValTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(CondVal->getType());
2260 if (!CondValTy || !match(CondVal, m_Constant(CondC)))
2261 return nullptr;
2262
2263 unsigned NumElts = CondValTy->getNumElements();
2264 SmallVector<int, 16> Mask;
2265 Mask.reserve(NumElts);
2266 for (unsigned i = 0; i != NumElts; ++i) {
2267 Constant *Elt = CondC->getAggregateElement(i);
2268 if (!Elt)
2269 return nullptr;
2270
2271 if (Elt->isOneValue()) {
2272 // If the select condition element is true, choose from the 1st vector.
2273 Mask.push_back(i);
2274 } else if (Elt->isNullValue()) {
2275 // If the select condition element is false, choose from the 2nd vector.
2276 Mask.push_back(i + NumElts);
2277 } else if (isa<UndefValue>(Elt)) {
2278 // Undef in a select condition (choose one of the operands) does not mean
2279 // the same thing as undef in a shuffle mask (any value is acceptable), so
2280 // give up.
2281 return nullptr;
2282 } else {
2283 // Bail out on a constant expression.
2284 return nullptr;
2285 }
2286 }
2287
2288 return new ShuffleVectorInst(SI.getTrueValue(), SI.getFalseValue(), Mask);
2289}
2290
2291/// If we have a select of vectors with a scalar condition, try to convert that
2292/// to a vector select by splatting the condition. A splat may get folded with
2293/// other operations in IR and having all operands of a select be vector types
2294/// is likely better for vector codegen.
2295static Instruction *canonicalizeScalarSelectOfVecs(SelectInst &Sel,
2296 InstCombinerImpl &IC) {
2297 auto *Ty = dyn_cast<VectorType>(Sel.getType());
2298 if (!Ty)
2299 return nullptr;
2300
2301 // We can replace a single-use extract with constant index.
2302 Value *Cond = Sel.getCondition();
2303 if (!match(Cond, m_OneUse(m_ExtractElt(m_Value(), m_ConstantInt()))))
2304 return nullptr;
2305
2306 // select (extelt V, Index), T, F --> select (splat V, Index), T, F
2307 // Splatting the extracted condition reduces code (we could directly create a
2308 // splat shuffle of the source vector to eliminate the intermediate step).
2309 return IC.replaceOperand(
2310 Sel, 0, IC.Builder.CreateVectorSplat(Ty->getElementCount(), Cond));
2311}
2312
2313/// Reuse bitcasted operands between a compare and select:
2314/// select (cmp (bitcast C), (bitcast D)), (bitcast' C), (bitcast' D) -->
2315/// bitcast (select (cmp (bitcast C), (bitcast D)), (bitcast C), (bitcast D))
2316static Instruction *foldSelectCmpBitcasts(SelectInst &Sel,
2317 InstCombiner::BuilderTy &Builder) {
2318 Value *Cond = Sel.getCondition();
2319 Value *TVal = Sel.getTrueValue();
2320 Value *FVal = Sel.getFalseValue();
2321
2322 CmpPredicate Pred;
2323 Value *A, *B;
2324 if (!match(Cond, m_Cmp(Pred, m_Value(A), m_Value(B))))
2325 return nullptr;
2326
2327 // The select condition is a compare instruction. If the select's true/false
2328 // values are already the same as the compare operands, there's nothing to do.
2329 if (TVal == A || TVal == B || FVal == A || FVal == B)
2330 return nullptr;
2331
2332 Value *C, *D;
2333 if (!match(A, m_BitCast(m_Value(C))) || !match(B, m_BitCast(m_Value(D))))
2334 return nullptr;
2335
2336 // select (cmp (bitcast C), (bitcast D)), (bitcast TSrc), (bitcast FSrc)
2337 Value *TSrc, *FSrc;
2338 if (!match(TVal, m_BitCast(m_Value(TSrc))) ||
2339 !match(FVal, m_BitCast(m_Value(FSrc))))
2340 return nullptr;
2341
2342 // If the select true/false values are *different bitcasts* of the same source
2343 // operands, make the select operands the same as the compare operands and
2344 // cast the result. This is the canonical select form for min/max.
2345 Value *NewSel;
2346 if (TSrc == C && FSrc == D) {
2347 // select (cmp (bitcast C), (bitcast D)), (bitcast' C), (bitcast' D) -->
2348 // bitcast (select (cmp A, B), A, B)
2349 NewSel = Builder.CreateSelect(Cond, A, B, "", &Sel);
2350 } else if (TSrc == D && FSrc == C) {
2351 // select (cmp (bitcast C), (bitcast D)), (bitcast' D), (bitcast' C) -->
2352 // bitcast (select (cmp A, B), B, A)
2353 NewSel = Builder.CreateSelect(Cond, B, A, "", &Sel);
2354 } else {
2355 return nullptr;
2356 }
2357 return new BitCastInst(NewSel, Sel.getType());
2358}
2359
2360/// Try to eliminate select instructions that test the returned flag of cmpxchg
2361/// instructions.
2362///
2363/// If a select instruction tests the returned flag of a cmpxchg instruction and
2364/// selects between the returned value of the cmpxchg instruction its compare
2365/// operand, the result of the select will always be equal to its false value.
2366/// For example:
2367///
2368/// %cmpxchg = cmpxchg ptr %ptr, i64 %compare, i64 %new_value seq_cst seq_cst
2369/// %val = extractvalue { i64, i1 } %cmpxchg, 0
2370/// %success = extractvalue { i64, i1 } %cmpxchg, 1
2371/// %sel = select i1 %success, i64 %compare, i64 %val
2372/// ret i64 %sel
2373///
2374/// The returned value of the cmpxchg instruction (%val) is the original value
2375/// located at %ptr prior to any update. If the cmpxchg operation succeeds, %val
2376/// must have been equal to %compare. Thus, the result of the select is always
2377/// equal to %val, and the code can be simplified to:
2378///
2379/// %cmpxchg = cmpxchg ptr %ptr, i64 %compare, i64 %new_value seq_cst seq_cst
2380/// %val = extractvalue { i64, i1 } %cmpxchg, 0
2381/// ret i64 %val
2382///
2383static Value *foldSelectCmpXchg(SelectInst &SI) {
2384 // A helper that determines if V is an extractvalue instruction whose
2385 // aggregate operand is a cmpxchg instruction and whose single index is equal
2386 // to I. If such conditions are true, the helper returns the cmpxchg
2387 // instruction; otherwise, a nullptr is returned.
2388 auto isExtractFromCmpXchg = [](Value *V, unsigned I) -> AtomicCmpXchgInst * {
2389 auto *Extract = dyn_cast<ExtractValueInst>(V);
2390 if (!Extract)
2391 return nullptr;
2392 if (Extract->getIndices()[0] != I)
2393 return nullptr;
2394 return dyn_cast<AtomicCmpXchgInst>(Extract->getAggregateOperand());
2395 };
2396
2397 // If the select has a single user, and this user is a select instruction that
2398 // we can simplify, skip the cmpxchg simplification for now.
2399 if (SI.hasOneUse())
2400 if (auto *Select = dyn_cast<SelectInst>(SI.user_back()))
2401 if (Select->getCondition() == SI.getCondition())
2402 if (Select->getFalseValue() == SI.getTrueValue() ||
2403 Select->getTrueValue() == SI.getFalseValue())
2404 return nullptr;
2405
2406 // Ensure the select condition is the returned flag of a cmpxchg instruction.
2407 auto *CmpXchg = isExtractFromCmpXchg(SI.getCondition(), 1);
2408 if (!CmpXchg)
2409 return nullptr;
2410
2411 // Check the true value case: The true value of the select is the returned
2412 // value of the same cmpxchg used by the condition, and the false value is the
2413 // cmpxchg instruction's compare operand.
2414 if (auto *X = isExtractFromCmpXchg(SI.getTrueValue(), 0))
2415 if (X == CmpXchg && X->getCompareOperand() == SI.getFalseValue())
2416 return SI.getFalseValue();
2417
2418 // Check the false value case: The false value of the select is the returned
2419 // value of the same cmpxchg used by the condition, and the true value is the
2420 // cmpxchg instruction's compare operand.
2421 if (auto *X = isExtractFromCmpXchg(SI.getFalseValue(), 0))
2422 if (X == CmpXchg && X->getCompareOperand() == SI.getTrueValue())
2423 return SI.getFalseValue();
2424
2425 return nullptr;
2426}
2427
2428/// Try to reduce a funnel/rotate pattern that includes a compare and select
2429/// into a funnel shift intrinsic. Example:
2430/// rotl32(a, b) --> (b == 0 ? a : ((a >> (32 - b)) | (a << b)))
2431/// --> call llvm.fshl.i32(a, a, b)
2432/// fshl32(a, b, c) --> (c == 0 ? a : ((b >> (32 - c)) | (a << c)))
2433/// --> call llvm.fshl.i32(a, b, c)
2434/// fshr32(a, b, c) --> (c == 0 ? b : ((a >> (32 - c)) | (b << c)))
2435/// --> call llvm.fshr.i32(a, b, c)
2436static Instruction *foldSelectFunnelShift(SelectInst &Sel,
2437 InstCombiner::BuilderTy &Builder) {
2438 // This must be a power-of-2 type for a bitmasking transform to be valid.
2439 unsigned Width = Sel.getType()->getScalarSizeInBits();
2440 if (!isPowerOf2_32(Width))
2441 return nullptr;
2442
2443 BinaryOperator *Or0, *Or1;
2444 if (!match(Sel.getFalseValue(), m_OneUse(m_Or(m_BinOp(Or0), m_BinOp(Or1)))))
2445 return nullptr;
2446
2447 Value *SV0, *SV1, *SA0, *SA1;
2448 if (!match(Or0, m_OneUse(m_LogicalShift(m_Value(SV0),
2449 m_ZExtOrSelf(m_Value(SA0))))) ||
2450 !match(Or1, m_OneUse(m_LogicalShift(m_Value(SV1),
2451 m_ZExtOrSelf(m_Value(SA1))))) ||
2452 Or0->getOpcode() == Or1->getOpcode())
2453 return nullptr;
2454
2455 // Canonicalize to or(shl(SV0, SA0), lshr(SV1, SA1)).
2456 if (Or0->getOpcode() == BinaryOperator::LShr) {
2457 std::swap(Or0, Or1);
2458 std::swap(SV0, SV1);
2459 std::swap(SA0, SA1);
2460 }
2461 assert(Or0->getOpcode() == BinaryOperator::Shl &&
2462 Or1->getOpcode() == BinaryOperator::LShr &&
2463 "Illegal or(shift,shift) pair");
2464
2465 // Check the shift amounts to see if they are an opposite pair.
2466 Value *ShAmt;
2467 if (match(SA1, m_OneUse(m_Sub(m_SpecificInt(Width), m_Specific(SA0)))))
2468 ShAmt = SA0;
2469 else if (match(SA0, m_OneUse(m_Sub(m_SpecificInt(Width), m_Specific(SA1)))))
2470 ShAmt = SA1;
2471 else
2472 return nullptr;
2473
2474 // We should now have this pattern:
2475 // select ?, TVal, (or (shl SV0, SA0), (lshr SV1, SA1))
2476 // The false value of the select must be a funnel-shift of the true value:
2477 // IsFShl -> TVal must be SV0 else TVal must be SV1.
2478 bool IsFshl = (ShAmt == SA0);
2479 Value *TVal = Sel.getTrueValue();
2480 if ((IsFshl && TVal != SV0) || (!IsFshl && TVal != SV1))
2481 return nullptr;
2482
2483 // Finally, see if the select is filtering out a shift-by-zero.
2484 Value *Cond = Sel.getCondition();
2485 if (!match(Cond, m_OneUse(m_SpecificICmp(ICmpInst::ICMP_EQ, m_Specific(ShAmt),
2486 m_ZeroInt()))))
2487 return nullptr;
2488
2489 // If this is not a rotate then the select was blocking poison from the
2490 // 'shift-by-zero' non-TVal, but a funnel shift won't - so freeze it.
2491 if (SV0 != SV1) {
2492 if (IsFshl && !llvm::isGuaranteedNotToBePoison(SV1))
2493 SV1 = Builder.CreateFreeze(SV1);
2494 else if (!IsFshl && !llvm::isGuaranteedNotToBePoison(SV0))
2495 SV0 = Builder.CreateFreeze(SV0);
2496 }
2497
2498 // This is a funnel/rotate that avoids shift-by-bitwidth UB in a suboptimal way.
2499 // Convert to funnel shift intrinsic.
2500 Intrinsic::ID IID = IsFshl ? Intrinsic::fshl : Intrinsic::fshr;
2501 Function *F =
2502 Intrinsic::getOrInsertDeclaration(Sel.getModule(), IID, Sel.getType());
2503 ShAmt = Builder.CreateZExt(ShAmt, Sel.getType());
2504 return CallInst::Create(F, { SV0, SV1, ShAmt });
2505}
2506
2507static Instruction *foldSelectToCopysign(SelectInst &Sel,
2508 InstCombiner::BuilderTy &Builder) {
2509 Value *Cond = Sel.getCondition();
2510 Value *TVal = Sel.getTrueValue();
2511 Value *FVal = Sel.getFalseValue();
2512 Type *SelType = Sel.getType();
2513
2514 // Match select ?, TC, FC where the constants are equal but negated.
2515 // TODO: Generalize to handle a negated variable operand?
2516 const APFloat *TC, *FC;
2517 if (!match(TVal, m_APFloatAllowPoison(TC)) ||
2518 !match(FVal, m_APFloatAllowPoison(FC)) ||
2519 !abs(*TC).bitwiseIsEqual(abs(*FC)))
2520 return nullptr;
2521
2522 assert(TC != FC && "Expected equal select arms to simplify");
2523
2524 Value *X;
2525 const APInt *C;
2526 bool IsTrueIfSignSet;
2527 CmpPredicate Pred;
2528 if (!match(Cond, m_OneUse(m_ICmp(Pred, m_ElementWiseBitCast(m_Value(X)),
2529 m_APInt(C)))) ||
2530 !isSignBitCheck(Pred, *C, IsTrueIfSignSet) || X->getType() != SelType)
2531 return nullptr;
2532
2533 // If needed, negate the value that will be the sign argument of the copysign:
2534 // (bitcast X) < 0 ? -TC : TC --> copysign(TC, X)
2535 // (bitcast X) < 0 ? TC : -TC --> copysign(TC, -X)
2536 // (bitcast X) >= 0 ? -TC : TC --> copysign(TC, -X)
2537 // (bitcast X) >= 0 ? TC : -TC --> copysign(TC, X)
2538 // Note: FMF from the select can not be propagated to the new instructions.
2539 if (IsTrueIfSignSet ^ TC->isNegative())
2540 X = Builder.CreateFNeg(X);
2541
2542 // Canonicalize the magnitude argument as the positive constant since we do
2543 // not care about its sign.
2544 Value *MagArg = ConstantFP::get(SelType, abs(*TC));
2545 Function *F = Intrinsic::getOrInsertDeclaration(
2546 Sel.getModule(), Intrinsic::copysign, Sel.getType());
2547 return CallInst::Create(F, { MagArg, X });
2548}
2549
2550Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::foldVectorSelect(SelectInst &Sel) {
2551 if (!isa<VectorType>(Sel.getType()))
2552 return nullptr;
2553
2554 Value *Cond = Sel.getCondition();
2555 Value *TVal = Sel.getTrueValue();
2556 Value *FVal = Sel.getFalseValue();
2557 Value *C, *X, *Y;
2558
2559 if (match(Cond, m_VecReverse(m_Value(C)))) {
2560 auto createSelReverse = [&](Value *C, Value *X, Value *Y) {
2561 Value *V = Builder.CreateSelect(C, X, Y, Sel.getName(), &Sel);
2562 if (auto *I = dyn_cast<Instruction>(V))
2563 I->copyIRFlags(&Sel);
2564 Module *M = Sel.getModule();
2565 Function *F = Intrinsic::getOrInsertDeclaration(
2566 M, Intrinsic::vector_reverse, V->getType());
2567 return CallInst::Create(F, V);
2568 };
2569
2570 if (match(TVal, m_VecReverse(m_Value(X)))) {
2571 // select rev(C), rev(X), rev(Y) --> rev(select C, X, Y)
2572 if (match(FVal, m_VecReverse(m_Value(Y))) &&
2573 (Cond->hasOneUse() || TVal->hasOneUse() || FVal->hasOneUse()))
2574 return createSelReverse(C, X, Y);
2575
2576 // select rev(C), rev(X), FValSplat --> rev(select C, X, FValSplat)
2577 if ((Cond->hasOneUse() || TVal->hasOneUse()) && isSplatValue(FVal))
2578 return createSelReverse(C, X, FVal);
2579 }
2580 // select rev(C), TValSplat, rev(Y) --> rev(select C, TValSplat, Y)
2581 else if (isSplatValue(TVal) && match(FVal, m_VecReverse(m_Value(Y))) &&
2582 (Cond->hasOneUse() || FVal->hasOneUse()))
2583 return createSelReverse(C, TVal, Y);
2584 }
2585
2586 auto *VecTy = dyn_cast<FixedVectorType>(Sel.getType());
2587 if (!VecTy)
2588 return nullptr;
2589
2590 unsigned NumElts = VecTy->getNumElements();
2591 APInt PoisonElts(NumElts, 0);
2592 APInt AllOnesEltMask(APInt::getAllOnes(NumElts));
2593 if (Value *V = SimplifyDemandedVectorElts(&Sel, AllOnesEltMask, PoisonElts)) {
2594 if (V != &Sel)
2595 return replaceInstUsesWith(Sel, V);
2596 return &Sel;
2597 }
2598
2599 // A select of a "select shuffle" with a common operand can be rearranged
2600 // to select followed by "select shuffle". Because of poison, this only works
2601 // in the case of a shuffle with no undefined mask elements.
2602 ArrayRef<int> Mask;
2603 if (match(TVal, m_OneUse(m_Shuffle(m_Value(X), m_Value(Y), m_Mask(Mask)))) &&
2604 !is_contained(Mask, PoisonMaskElem) &&
2605 cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(TVal)->isSelect()) {
2606 if (X == FVal) {
2607 // select Cond, (shuf_sel X, Y), X --> shuf_sel X, (select Cond, Y, X)
2608 Value *NewSel = Builder.CreateSelect(Cond, Y, X, "sel", &Sel);
2609 return new ShuffleVectorInst(X, NewSel, Mask);
2610 }
2611 if (Y == FVal) {
2612 // select Cond, (shuf_sel X, Y), Y --> shuf_sel (select Cond, X, Y), Y
2613 Value *NewSel = Builder.CreateSelect(Cond, X, Y, "sel", &Sel);
2614 return new ShuffleVectorInst(NewSel, Y, Mask);
2615 }
2616 }
2617 if (match(FVal, m_OneUse(m_Shuffle(m_Value(X), m_Value(Y), m_Mask(Mask)))) &&
2618 !is_contained(Mask, PoisonMaskElem) &&
2619 cast<ShuffleVectorInst>(FVal)->isSelect()) {
2620 if (X == TVal) {
2621 // select Cond, X, (shuf_sel X, Y) --> shuf_sel X, (select Cond, X, Y)
2622 Value *NewSel = Builder.CreateSelect(Cond, X, Y, "sel", &Sel);
2623 return new ShuffleVectorInst(X, NewSel, Mask);
2624 }
2625 if (Y == TVal) {
2626 // select Cond, Y, (shuf_sel X, Y) --> shuf_sel (select Cond, Y, X), Y
2627 Value *NewSel = Builder.CreateSelect(Cond, Y, X, "sel", &Sel);
2628 return new ShuffleVectorInst(NewSel, Y, Mask);
2629 }
2630 }
2631
2632 return nullptr;
2633}
2634
2635static Instruction *foldSelectToPhiImpl(SelectInst &Sel, BasicBlock *BB,
2636 const DominatorTree &DT,
2637 InstCombiner::BuilderTy &Builder) {
2638 // Find the block's immediate dominator that ends with a conditional branch
2639 // that matches select's condition (maybe inverted).
2640 auto *IDomNode = DT[BB]->getIDom();
2641 if (!IDomNode)
2642 return nullptr;
2643 BasicBlock *IDom = IDomNode->getBlock();
2644
2645 Value *Cond = Sel.getCondition();
2646 Value *IfTrue, *IfFalse;
2647 BasicBlock *TrueSucc, *FalseSucc;
2648 if (match(IDom->getTerminator(),
2649 m_Br(m_Specific(Cond), m_BasicBlock(TrueSucc),
2650 m_BasicBlock(FalseSucc)))) {
2651 IfTrue = Sel.getTrueValue();
2652 IfFalse = Sel.getFalseValue();
2653 } else if (match(IDom->getTerminator(),
2654 m_Br(m_Not(m_Specific(Cond)), m_BasicBlock(TrueSucc),
2655 m_BasicBlock(FalseSucc)))) {
2656 IfTrue = Sel.getFalseValue();
2657 IfFalse = Sel.getTrueValue();
2658 } else
2659 return nullptr;
2660
2661 // Make sure the branches are actually different.
2662 if (TrueSucc == FalseSucc)
2663 return nullptr;
2664
2665 // We want to replace select %cond, %a, %b with a phi that takes value %a
2666 // for all incoming edges that are dominated by condition `%cond == true`,
2667 // and value %b for edges dominated by condition `%cond == false`. If %a
2668 // or %b are also phis from the same basic block, we can go further and take
2669 // their incoming values from the corresponding blocks.
2670 BasicBlockEdge TrueEdge(IDom, TrueSucc);
2671 BasicBlockEdge FalseEdge(IDom, FalseSucc);
2672 DenseMap<BasicBlock *, Value *> Inputs;
2673 for (auto *Pred : predecessors(BB)) {
2674 // Check implication.
2675 BasicBlockEdge Incoming(Pred, BB);
2676 if (DT.dominates(TrueEdge, Incoming))
2677 Inputs[Pred] = IfTrue->DoPHITranslation(BB, Pred);
2678 else if (DT.dominates(FalseEdge, Incoming))
2679 Inputs[Pred] = IfFalse->DoPHITranslation(BB, Pred);
2680 else
2681 return nullptr;
2682 // Check availability.
2683 if (auto *Insn = dyn_cast<Instruction>(Inputs[Pred]))
2684 if (!DT.dominates(Insn, Pred->getTerminator()))
2685 return nullptr;
2686 }
2687
2688 Builder.SetInsertPoint(BB, BB->begin());
2689 auto *PN = Builder.CreatePHI(Sel.getType(), Inputs.size());
2690 for (auto *Pred : predecessors(BB))
2691 PN->addIncoming(Inputs[Pred], Pred);
2692 PN->takeName(&Sel);
2693 return PN;
2694}
2695
2696static Instruction *foldSelectToPhi(SelectInst &Sel, const DominatorTree &DT,
2697 InstCombiner::BuilderTy &Builder) {
2698 // Try to replace this select with Phi in one of these blocks.
2699 SmallSetVector<BasicBlock *, 4> CandidateBlocks;
2700 CandidateBlocks.insert(Sel.getParent());
2701 for (Value *V : Sel.operands())
2702 if (auto *I = dyn_cast<Instruction>(V))
2703 CandidateBlocks.insert(I->getParent());
2704
2705 for (BasicBlock *BB : CandidateBlocks)
2706 if (auto *PN = foldSelectToPhiImpl(Sel, BB, DT, Builder))
2707 return PN;
2708 return nullptr;
2709}
2710
2711/// Tries to reduce a pattern that arises when calculating the remainder of the
2712/// Euclidean division. When the divisor is a power of two and is guaranteed not
2713/// to be negative, a signed remainder can be folded with a bitwise and.
2714///
2715/// (x % n) < 0 ? (x % n) + n : (x % n)
2716/// -> x & (n - 1)
2717static Instruction *foldSelectWithSRem(SelectInst &SI, InstCombinerImpl &IC,
2718 IRBuilderBase &Builder) {
2719 Value *CondVal = SI.getCondition();
2720 Value *TrueVal = SI.getTrueValue();
2721 Value *FalseVal = SI.getFalseValue();
2722
2723 CmpPredicate Pred;
2724 Value *Op, *RemRes, *Remainder;
2725 const APInt *C;
2726 bool TrueIfSigned = false;
2727
2728 if (!(match(CondVal, m_ICmp(Pred, m_Value(RemRes), m_APInt(C))) &&
2729 isSignBitCheck(Pred, *C, TrueIfSigned)))
2730 return nullptr;
2731
2732 // If the sign bit is not set, we have a SGE/SGT comparison, and the operands
2733 // of the select are inverted.
2734 if (!TrueIfSigned)
2735 std::swap(TrueVal, FalseVal);
2736
2737 auto FoldToBitwiseAnd = [&](Value *Remainder) -> Instruction * {
2738 Value *Add = Builder.CreateAdd(
2739 Remainder, Constant::getAllOnesValue(RemRes->getType()));
2740 return BinaryOperator::CreateAnd(Op, Add);
2741 };
2742
2743 // Match the general case:
2744 // %rem = srem i32 %x, %n
2745 // %cnd = icmp slt i32 %rem, 0
2746 // %add = add i32 %rem, %n
2747 // %sel = select i1 %cnd, i32 %add, i32 %rem
2748 if (match(TrueVal, m_c_Add(m_Specific(RemRes), m_Value(Remainder))) &&
2749 match(RemRes, m_SRem(m_Value(Op), m_Specific(Remainder))) &&
2750 IC.isKnownToBeAPowerOfTwo(Remainder, /*OrZero=*/true) &&
2751 FalseVal == RemRes)
2752 return FoldToBitwiseAnd(Remainder);
2753
2754 // Match the case where the one arm has been replaced by constant 1:
2755 // %rem = srem i32 %n, 2
2756 // %cnd = icmp slt i32 %rem, 0
2757 // %sel = select i1 %cnd, i32 1, i32 %rem
2758 if (match(TrueVal, m_One()) &&
2759 match(RemRes, m_SRem(m_Value(Op), m_SpecificInt(2))) &&
2760 FalseVal == RemRes)
2761 return FoldToBitwiseAnd(ConstantInt::get(RemRes->getType(), 2));
2762
2763 return nullptr;
2764}
2765
2766static Value *foldSelectWithFrozenICmp(SelectInst &Sel, InstCombiner::BuilderTy &Builder) {
2767 FreezeInst *FI = dyn_cast<FreezeInst>(Sel.getCondition());
2768 if (!FI)
2769 return nullptr;
2770
2771 Value *Cond = FI->getOperand(0);
2772 Value *TrueVal = Sel.getTrueValue(), *FalseVal = Sel.getFalseValue();
2773
2774 // select (freeze(x == y)), x, y --> y
2775 // select (freeze(x != y)), x, y --> x
2776 // The freeze should be only used by this select. Otherwise, remaining uses of
2777 // the freeze can observe a contradictory value.
2778 // c = freeze(x == y) ; Let's assume that y = poison & x = 42; c is 0 or 1
2779 // a = select c, x, y ;
2780 // f(a, c) ; f(poison, 1) cannot happen, but if a is folded
2781 // ; to y, this can happen.
2782 CmpPredicate Pred;
2783 if (FI->hasOneUse() &&
2784 match(Cond, m_c_ICmp(Pred, m_Specific(TrueVal), m_Specific(FalseVal))) &&
2785 (Pred == ICmpInst::ICMP_EQ || Pred == ICmpInst::ICMP_NE)) {
2786 return Pred == ICmpInst::ICMP_EQ ? FalseVal : TrueVal;
2787 }
2788
2789 return nullptr;
2790}
2791
2792/// Given that \p CondVal is known to be \p CondIsTrue, try to simplify \p SI.
2793static Value *simplifyNestedSelectsUsingImpliedCond(SelectInst &SI,
2794 Value *CondVal,
2795 bool CondIsTrue,
2796 const DataLayout &DL) {
2797 Value *InnerCondVal = SI.getCondition();
2798 Value *InnerTrueVal = SI.getTrueValue();
2799 Value *InnerFalseVal = SI.getFalseValue();
2800 assert(CondVal->getType() == InnerCondVal->getType() &&
2801 "The type of inner condition must match with the outer.");
2802 if (auto Implied = isImpliedCondition(CondVal, InnerCondVal, DL, CondIsTrue))
2803 return *Implied ? InnerTrueVal : InnerFalseVal;
2804 return nullptr;
2805}
2806
2807Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::foldAndOrOfSelectUsingImpliedCond(Value *Op,
2808 SelectInst &SI,
2809 bool IsAnd) {
2810 assert(Op->getType()->isIntOrIntVectorTy(1) &&
2811 "Op must be either i1 or vector of i1.");
2812 if (SI.getCondition()->getType() != Op->getType())
2813 return nullptr;
2814 if (Value *V = simplifyNestedSelectsUsingImpliedCond(SI, Op, IsAnd, DL))
2815 return SelectInst::Create(Op,
2816 IsAnd ? V : ConstantInt::getTrue(Op->getType()),
2817 IsAnd ? ConstantInt::getFalse(Op->getType()) : V);
2818 return nullptr;
2819}
2820
2821// Canonicalize select with fcmp to fabs(). -0.0 makes this tricky. We need
2822// fast-math-flags (nsz) or fsub with +0.0 (not fneg) for this to work.
2823static Instruction *foldSelectWithFCmpToFabs(SelectInst &SI,
2824 InstCombinerImpl &IC) {
2825 Value *CondVal = SI.getCondition();
2826
2827 bool ChangedFMF = false;
2828 for (bool Swap : {false, true}) {
2829 Value *TrueVal = SI.getTrueValue();
2830 Value *X = SI.getFalseValue();
2831 CmpPredicate Pred;
2832
2833 if (Swap)
2834 std::swap(TrueVal, X);
2835
2836 if (!match(CondVal, m_FCmp(Pred, m_Specific(X), m_AnyZeroFP())))
2837 continue;
2838
2839 // fold (X <= +/-0.0) ? (0.0 - X) : X to fabs(X), when 'Swap' is false
2840 // fold (X > +/-0.0) ? X : (0.0 - X) to fabs(X), when 'Swap' is true
2841 if (match(TrueVal, m_FSub(m_PosZeroFP(), m_Specific(X)))) {
2842 if (!Swap && (Pred == FCmpInst::FCMP_OLE || Pred == FCmpInst::FCMP_ULE)) {
2843 Value *Fabs = IC.Builder.CreateUnaryIntrinsic(Intrinsic::fabs, X, &SI);
2844 return IC.replaceInstUsesWith(SI, Fabs);
2845 }
2846 if (Swap && (Pred == FCmpInst::FCMP_OGT || Pred == FCmpInst::FCMP_UGT)) {
2847 Value *Fabs = IC.Builder.CreateUnaryIntrinsic(Intrinsic::fabs, X, &SI);
2848 return IC.replaceInstUsesWith(SI, Fabs);
2849 }
2850 }
2851
2852 if (!match(TrueVal, m_FNeg(m_Specific(X))))
2853 return nullptr;
2854
2855 // Forward-propagate nnan and ninf from the fcmp to the select.
2856 // If all inputs are not those values, then the select is not either.
2857 // Note: nsz is defined differently, so it may not be correct to propagate.
2858 FastMathFlags FMF = cast<FPMathOperator>(CondVal)->getFastMathFlags();
2859 if (FMF.noNaNs() && !SI.hasNoNaNs()) {
2860 SI.setHasNoNaNs(true);
2861 ChangedFMF = true;
2862 }
2863 if (FMF.noInfs() && !SI.hasNoInfs()) {
2864 SI.setHasNoInfs(true);
2865 ChangedFMF = true;
2866 }
2867 // Forward-propagate nnan from the fneg to the select.
2868 // The nnan flag can be propagated iff fneg is selected when X is NaN.
2869 if (!SI.hasNoNaNs() && cast<FPMathOperator>(TrueVal)->hasNoNaNs() &&
2870 (Swap ? FCmpInst::isOrdered(Pred) : FCmpInst::isUnordered(Pred))) {
2871 SI.setHasNoNaNs(true);
2872 ChangedFMF = true;
2873 }
2874
2875 // With nsz, when 'Swap' is false:
2876 // fold (X < +/-0.0) ? -X : X or (X <= +/-0.0) ? -X : X to fabs(X)
2877 // fold (X > +/-0.0) ? -X : X or (X >= +/-0.0) ? -X : X to -fabs(x)
2878 // when 'Swap' is true:
2879 // fold (X > +/-0.0) ? X : -X or (X >= +/-0.0) ? X : -X to fabs(X)
2880 // fold (X < +/-0.0) ? X : -X or (X <= +/-0.0) ? X : -X to -fabs(X)
2881 //
2882 // Note: We require "nnan" for this fold because fcmp ignores the signbit
2883 // of NAN, but IEEE-754 specifies the signbit of NAN values with
2884 // fneg/fabs operations.
2885 if (!SI.hasNoSignedZeros() || !SI.hasNoNaNs())
2886 return nullptr;
2887
2888 if (Swap)
2889 Pred = FCmpInst::getSwappedPredicate(Pred);
2890
2891 bool IsLTOrLE = Pred == FCmpInst::FCMP_OLT || Pred == FCmpInst::FCMP_OLE ||
2892 Pred == FCmpInst::FCMP_ULT || Pred == FCmpInst::FCMP_ULE;
2893 bool IsGTOrGE = Pred == FCmpInst::FCMP_OGT || Pred == FCmpInst::FCMP_OGE ||
2894 Pred == FCmpInst::FCMP_UGT || Pred == FCmpInst::FCMP_UGE;
2895
2896 if (IsLTOrLE) {
2897 Value *Fabs = IC.Builder.CreateUnaryIntrinsic(Intrinsic::fabs, X, &SI);
2898 return IC.replaceInstUsesWith(SI, Fabs);
2899 }
2900 if (IsGTOrGE) {
2901 Value *Fabs = IC.Builder.CreateUnaryIntrinsic(Intrinsic::fabs, X, &SI);
2902 Instruction *NewFNeg = UnaryOperator::CreateFNeg(Fabs);
2903 NewFNeg->setFastMathFlags(SI.getFastMathFlags());
2904 return NewFNeg;
2905 }
2906 }
2907
2908 // Match select with (icmp slt (bitcast X to int), 0)
2909 // or (icmp sgt (bitcast X to int), -1)
2910
2911 for (bool Swap : {false, true}) {
2912 Value *TrueVal = SI.getTrueValue();
2913 Value *X = SI.getFalseValue();
2914
2915 if (Swap)
2916 std::swap(TrueVal, X);
2917
2918 CmpPredicate Pred;
2919 const APInt *C;
2920 bool TrueIfSigned;
2921 if (!match(CondVal,
2922 m_ICmp(Pred, m_ElementWiseBitCast(m_Specific(X)), m_APInt(C))) ||
2923 !isSignBitCheck(Pred, *C, TrueIfSigned))
2924 continue;
2925 if (!match(TrueVal, m_FNeg(m_Specific(X))))
2926 return nullptr;
2927 if (Swap == TrueIfSigned && !CondVal->hasOneUse() && !TrueVal->hasOneUse())
2928 return nullptr;
2929
2930 // Fold (IsNeg ? -X : X) or (!IsNeg ? X : -X) to fabs(X)
2931 // Fold (IsNeg ? X : -X) or (!IsNeg ? -X : X) to -fabs(X)
2932 Value *Fabs = IC.Builder.CreateUnaryIntrinsic(Intrinsic::fabs, X, &SI);
2933 if (Swap != TrueIfSigned)
2934 return IC.replaceInstUsesWith(SI, Fabs);
2935 return UnaryOperator::CreateFNegFMF(Fabs, &SI);
2936 }
2937
2938 return ChangedFMF ? &SI : nullptr;
2939}
2940
2941// Match the following IR pattern:
2942// %x.lowbits = and i8 %x, %lowbitmask
2943// %x.lowbits.are.zero = icmp eq i8 %x.lowbits, 0
2944// %x.biased = add i8 %x, %bias
2945// %x.biased.highbits = and i8 %x.biased, %highbitmask
2946// %x.roundedup = select i1 %x.lowbits.are.zero, i8 %x, i8 %x.biased.highbits
2947// Define:
2948// %alignment = add i8 %lowbitmask, 1
2949// Iff 1. an %alignment is a power-of-two (aka, %lowbitmask is a low bit mask)
2950// and 2. %bias is equal to either %lowbitmask or %alignment,
2951// and 3. %highbitmask is equal to ~%lowbitmask (aka, to -%alignment)
2952// then this pattern can be transformed into:
2953// %x.offset = add i8 %x, %lowbitmask
2954// %x.roundedup = and i8 %x.offset, %highbitmask
2955static Value *
2956foldRoundUpIntegerWithPow2Alignment(SelectInst &SI,
2957 InstCombiner::BuilderTy &Builder) {
2958 Value *Cond = SI.getCondition();
2959 Value *X = SI.getTrueValue();
2960 Value *XBiasedHighBits = SI.getFalseValue();
2961
2962 CmpPredicate Pred;
2963 Value *XLowBits;
2964 if (!match(Cond, m_ICmp(Pred, m_Value(XLowBits), m_ZeroInt())) ||
2965 !ICmpInst::isEquality(Pred))
2966 return nullptr;
2967
2968 if (Pred == ICmpInst::Predicate::ICMP_NE)
2969 std::swap(X, XBiasedHighBits);
2970
2971 // FIXME: we could support non non-splats here.
2972
2973 const APInt *LowBitMaskCst;
2974 if (!match(XLowBits, m_And(m_Specific(X), m_APIntAllowPoison(LowBitMaskCst))))
2975 return nullptr;
2976
2977 // Match even if the AND and ADD are swapped.
2978 const APInt *BiasCst, *HighBitMaskCst;
2979 if (!match(XBiasedHighBits,
2980 m_And(m_Add(m_Specific(X), m_APIntAllowPoison(BiasCst)),
2981 m_APIntAllowPoison(HighBitMaskCst))) &&
2982 !match(XBiasedHighBits,
2983 m_Add(m_And(m_Specific(X), m_APIntAllowPoison(HighBitMaskCst)),
2984 m_APIntAllowPoison(BiasCst))))
2985 return nullptr;
2986
2987 if (!LowBitMaskCst->isMask())
2988 return nullptr;
2989
2990 APInt InvertedLowBitMaskCst = ~*LowBitMaskCst;
2991 if (InvertedLowBitMaskCst != *HighBitMaskCst)
2992 return nullptr;
2993
2994 APInt AlignmentCst = *LowBitMaskCst + 1;
2995
2996 if (*BiasCst != AlignmentCst && *BiasCst != *LowBitMaskCst)
2997 return nullptr;
2998
2999 if (!XBiasedHighBits->hasOneUse()) {
3000 // We can't directly return XBiasedHighBits if it is more poisonous.
3001 if (*BiasCst == *LowBitMaskCst && impliesPoison(XBiasedHighBits, X))
3002 return XBiasedHighBits;
3003 return nullptr;
3004 }
3005
3006 // FIXME: could we preserve undef's here?
3007 Type *Ty = X->getType();
3008 Value *XOffset = Builder.CreateAdd(X, ConstantInt::get(Ty, *LowBitMaskCst),
3009 X->getName() + ".biased");
3010 Value *R = Builder.CreateAnd(XOffset, ConstantInt::get(Ty, *HighBitMaskCst));
3011 R->takeName(&SI);
3012 return R;
3013}
3014
3015namespace {
3016struct DecomposedSelect {
3017 Value *Cond = nullptr;
3018 Value *TrueVal = nullptr;
3019 Value *FalseVal = nullptr;
3020};
3021} // namespace
3022
3023/// Folds patterns like:
3024/// select c2 (select c1 a b) (select c1 b a)
3025/// into:
3026/// select (xor c1 c2) b a
3027static Instruction *
3028foldSelectOfSymmetricSelect(SelectInst &OuterSelVal,
3029 InstCombiner::BuilderTy &Builder) {
3030
3031 Value *OuterCond, *InnerCond, *InnerTrueVal, *InnerFalseVal;
3032 if (!match(
3033 &OuterSelVal,
3034 m_Select(m_Value(OuterCond),
3035 m_OneUse(m_Select(m_Value(InnerCond), m_Value(InnerTrueVal),
3036 m_Value(InnerFalseVal))),
3037 m_OneUse(m_Select(m_Deferred(InnerCond),
3038 m_Deferred(InnerFalseVal),
3039 m_Deferred(InnerTrueVal))))))
3040 return nullptr;
3041
3042 if (OuterCond->getType() != InnerCond->getType())
3043 return nullptr;
3044
3045 Value *Xor = Builder.CreateXor(InnerCond, OuterCond);
3046 return SelectInst::Create(Xor, InnerFalseVal, InnerTrueVal);
3047}
3048
3049/// Look for patterns like
3050/// %outer.cond = select i1 %inner.cond, i1 %alt.cond, i1 false
3051/// %inner.sel = select i1 %inner.cond, i8 %inner.sel.t, i8 %inner.sel.f
3052/// %outer.sel = select i1 %outer.cond, i8 %outer.sel.t, i8 %inner.sel
3053/// and rewrite it as
3054/// %inner.sel = select i1 %cond.alternative, i8 %sel.outer.t, i8 %sel.inner.t
3055/// %sel.outer = select i1 %cond.inner, i8 %inner.sel, i8 %sel.inner.f
3056static Instruction *foldNestedSelects(SelectInst &OuterSelVal,
3057 InstCombiner::BuilderTy &Builder) {
3058 // We must start with a `select`.
3059 DecomposedSelect OuterSel;
3060 match(&OuterSelVal,
3061 m_Select(m_Value(OuterSel.Cond), m_Value(OuterSel.TrueVal),
3062 m_Value(OuterSel.FalseVal)));
3063
3064 // Canonicalize inversion of the outermost `select`'s condition.
3065 if (match(OuterSel.Cond, m_Not(m_Value(OuterSel.Cond))))
3066 std::swap(OuterSel.TrueVal, OuterSel.FalseVal);
3067
3068 // The condition of the outermost select must be an `and`/`or`.
3069 if (!match(OuterSel.Cond, m_c_LogicalOp(m_Value(), m_Value())))
3070 return nullptr;
3071
3072 // Depending on the logical op, inner select might be in different hand.
3073 bool IsAndVariant = match(OuterSel.Cond, m_LogicalAnd());
3074 Value *InnerSelVal = IsAndVariant ? OuterSel.FalseVal : OuterSel.TrueVal;
3075
3076 // Profitability check - avoid increasing instruction count.
3077 if (none_of(ArrayRef<Value *>({OuterSelVal.getCondition(), InnerSelVal}),
3078 [](Value *V) { return V->hasOneUse(); }))
3079 return nullptr;
3080
3081 // The appropriate hand of the outermost `select` must be a select itself.
3082 DecomposedSelect InnerSel;
3083 if (!match(InnerSelVal,
3084 m_Select(m_Value(InnerSel.Cond), m_Value(InnerSel.TrueVal),
3085 m_Value(InnerSel.FalseVal))))
3086 return nullptr;
3087
3088 // Canonicalize inversion of the innermost `select`'s condition.
3089 if (match(InnerSel.Cond, m_Not(m_Value(InnerSel.Cond))))
3090 std::swap(InnerSel.TrueVal, InnerSel.FalseVal);
3091
3092 Value *AltCond = nullptr;
3093 auto matchOuterCond = [OuterSel, IsAndVariant, &AltCond](auto m_InnerCond) {
3094 // An unsimplified select condition can match both LogicalAnd and LogicalOr
3095 // (select true, true, false). Since below we assume that LogicalAnd implies
3096 // InnerSel match the FVal and vice versa for LogicalOr, we can't match the
3097 // alternative pattern here.
3098 return IsAndVariant ? match(OuterSel.Cond,
3099 m_c_LogicalAnd(m_InnerCond, m_Value(AltCond)))
3100 : match(OuterSel.Cond,
3101 m_c_LogicalOr(m_InnerCond, m_Value(AltCond)));
3102 };
3103
3104 // Finally, match the condition that was driving the outermost `select`,
3105 // it should be a logical operation between the condition that was driving
3106 // the innermost `select` (after accounting for the possible inversions
3107 // of the condition), and some other condition.
3108 if (matchOuterCond(m_Specific(InnerSel.Cond))) {
3109 // Done!
3110 } else if (Value * NotInnerCond; matchOuterCond(m_CombineAnd(
3111 m_Not(m_Specific(InnerSel.Cond)), m_Value(NotInnerCond)))) {
3112 // Done!
3113 std::swap(InnerSel.TrueVal, InnerSel.FalseVal);
3114 InnerSel.Cond = NotInnerCond;
3115 } else // Not the pattern we were looking for.
3116 return nullptr;
3117
3118 Value *SelInner = Builder.CreateSelect(
3119 AltCond, IsAndVariant ? OuterSel.TrueVal : InnerSel.FalseVal,
3120 IsAndVariant ? InnerSel.TrueVal : OuterSel.FalseVal);
3121 SelInner->takeName(InnerSelVal);
3122 return SelectInst::Create(InnerSel.Cond,
3123 IsAndVariant ? SelInner : InnerSel.TrueVal,
3124 !IsAndVariant ? SelInner : InnerSel.FalseVal);
3125}
3126
3127/// Return true if V is poison or \p Expected given that ValAssumedPoison is
3128/// already poison. For example, if ValAssumedPoison is `icmp samesign X, 10`
3129/// and V is `icmp ne X, 5`, impliesPoisonOrCond returns true.
3130static bool impliesPoisonOrCond(const Value *ValAssumedPoison, const Value *V,
3131 bool Expected) {
3132 if (impliesPoison(ValAssumedPoison, V))
3133 return true;
3134
3135 // Handle the case that ValAssumedPoison is `icmp samesign pred X, C1` and V
3136 // is `icmp pred X, C2`, where C1 is well-defined.
3137 if (auto *ICmp = dyn_cast<ICmpInst>(ValAssumedPoison)) {
3138 Value *LHS = ICmp->getOperand(0);
3139 const APInt *RHSC1;
3140 const APInt *RHSC2;
3141 CmpPredicate Pred;
3142 if (ICmp->hasSameSign() &&
3143 match(ICmp->getOperand(1), m_APIntForbidPoison(RHSC1)) &&
3144 match(V, m_ICmp(Pred, m_Specific(LHS), m_APIntAllowPoison(RHSC2)))) {
3145 unsigned BitWidth = RHSC1->getBitWidth();
3146 ConstantRange CRX =
3147 RHSC1->isNonNegative()
3148 ? ConstantRange(APInt::getSignedMinValue(BitWidth),
3149 APInt::getZero(BitWidth))
3150 : ConstantRange(APInt::getZero(BitWidth),
3151 APInt::getSignedMinValue(BitWidth));
3152 return CRX.icmp(Expected ? Pred : ICmpInst::getInverseCmpPredicate(Pred),
3153 *RHSC2);
3154 }
3155 }
3156
3157 return false;
3158}
3159
3160Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::foldSelectOfBools(SelectInst &SI) {
3161 Value *CondVal = SI.getCondition();
3162 Value *TrueVal = SI.getTrueValue();
3163 Value *FalseVal = SI.getFalseValue();
3164 Type *SelType = SI.getType();
3165
3166 // Avoid potential infinite loops by checking for non-constant condition.
3167 // TODO: Can we assert instead by improving canonicalizeSelectToShuffle()?
3168 // Scalar select must have simplified?
3169 if (!SelType->isIntOrIntVectorTy(1) || isa<Constant>(CondVal) ||
3170 TrueVal->getType() != CondVal->getType())
3171 return nullptr;
3172
3173 auto *One = ConstantInt::getTrue(SelType);
3174 auto *Zero = ConstantInt::getFalse(SelType);
3175 Value *A, *B, *C, *D;
3176
3177 // Folding select to and/or i1 isn't poison safe in general. impliesPoison
3178 // checks whether folding it does not convert a well-defined value into
3179 // poison.
3180 if (match(TrueVal, m_One())) {
3181 if (impliesPoisonOrCond(FalseVal, CondVal, /*Expected=*/false)) {
3182 // Change: A = select B, true, C --> A = or B, C
3183 return BinaryOperator::CreateOr(CondVal, FalseVal);
3184 }
3185
3186 if (match(CondVal, m_OneUse(m_Select(m_Value(A), m_One(), m_Value(B)))) &&
3187 impliesPoisonOrCond(FalseVal, B, /*Expected=*/false)) {
3188 // (A || B) || C --> A || (B | C)
3189 return replaceInstUsesWith(
3190 SI, Builder.CreateLogicalOr(A, Builder.CreateOr(B, FalseVal)));
3191 }
3192
3193 // (A && B) || (C && B) --> (A || C) && B
3194 if (match(CondVal, m_LogicalAnd(m_Value(A), m_Value(B))) &&
3195 match(FalseVal, m_LogicalAnd(m_Value(C), m_Value(D))) &&
3196 (CondVal->hasOneUse() || FalseVal->hasOneUse())) {
3197 bool CondLogicAnd = isa<SelectInst>(CondVal);
3198 bool FalseLogicAnd = isa<SelectInst>(FalseVal);
3199 auto AndFactorization = [&](Value *Common, Value *InnerCond,
3200 Value *InnerVal,
3201 bool SelFirst = false) -> Instruction * {
3202 Value *InnerSel = Builder.CreateSelect(InnerCond, One, InnerVal);
3203 if (SelFirst)
3204 std::swap(Common, InnerSel);
3205 if (FalseLogicAnd || (CondLogicAnd && Common == A))
3206 return SelectInst::Create(Common, InnerSel, Zero);
3207 else
3208 return BinaryOperator::CreateAnd(Common, InnerSel);
3209 };
3210
3211 if (A == C)
3212 return AndFactorization(A, B, D);
3213 if (A == D)
3214 return AndFactorization(A, B, C);
3215 if (B == C)
3216 return AndFactorization(B, A, D);
3217 if (B == D)
3218 return AndFactorization(B, A, C, CondLogicAnd && FalseLogicAnd);
3219 }
3220 }
3221
3222 if (match(FalseVal, m_Zero())) {
3223 if (impliesPoisonOrCond(TrueVal, CondVal, /*Expected=*/true)) {
3224 // Change: A = select B, C, false --> A = and B, C
3225 return BinaryOperator::CreateAnd(CondVal, TrueVal);
3226 }
3227
3228 if (match(CondVal, m_OneUse(m_Select(m_Value(A), m_Value(B), m_Zero()))) &&
3229 impliesPoisonOrCond(TrueVal, B, /*Expected=*/true)) {
3230 // (A && B) && C --> A && (B & C)
3231 return replaceInstUsesWith(
3232 SI, Builder.CreateLogicalAnd(A, Builder.CreateAnd(B, TrueVal)));
3233 }
3234
3235 // (A || B) && (C || B) --> (A && C) || B
3236 if (match(CondVal, m_LogicalOr(m_Value(A), m_Value(B))) &&
3237 match(TrueVal, m_LogicalOr(m_Value(C), m_Value(D))) &&
3238 (CondVal->hasOneUse() || TrueVal->hasOneUse())) {
3239 bool CondLogicOr = isa<SelectInst>(CondVal);
3240 bool TrueLogicOr = isa<SelectInst>(TrueVal);
3241 auto OrFactorization = [&](Value *Common, Value *InnerCond,
3242 Value *InnerVal,
3243 bool SelFirst = false) -> Instruction * {
3244 Value *InnerSel = Builder.CreateSelect(InnerCond, InnerVal, Zero);
3245 if (SelFirst)
3246 std::swap(Common, InnerSel);
3247 if (TrueLogicOr || (CondLogicOr && Common == A))
3248 return SelectInst::Create(Common, One, InnerSel);
3249 else
3250 return BinaryOperator::CreateOr(Common, InnerSel);
3251 };
3252
3253 if (A == C)
3254 return OrFactorization(A, B, D);
3255 if (A == D)
3256 return OrFactorization(A, B, C);
3257 if (B == C)
3258 return OrFactorization(B, A, D);
3259 if (B == D)
3260 return OrFactorization(B, A, C, CondLogicOr && TrueLogicOr);
3261 }
3262 }
3263
3264 // We match the "full" 0 or 1 constant here to avoid a potential infinite
3265 // loop with vectors that may have undefined/poison elements.
3266 // select a, false, b -> select !a, b, false
3267 if (match(TrueVal, m_Specific(Zero))) {
3268 Value *NotCond = Builder.CreateNot(CondVal, "not." + CondVal->getName());
3269 return SelectInst::Create(NotCond, FalseVal, Zero);
3270 }
3271 // select a, b, true -> select !a, true, b
3272 if (match(FalseVal, m_Specific(One))) {
3273 Value *NotCond = Builder.CreateNot(CondVal, "not." + CondVal->getName());
3274 return SelectInst::Create(NotCond, One, TrueVal);
3275 }
3276
3277 // DeMorgan in select form: !a && !b --> !(a || b)
3278 // select !a, !b, false --> not (select a, true, b)
3279 if (match(&SI, m_LogicalAnd(m_Not(m_Value(A)), m_Not(m_Value(B)))) &&
3280 (CondVal->hasOneUse() || TrueVal->hasOneUse()) &&
3281 !match(A, m_ConstantExpr()) && !match(B, m_ConstantExpr()))
3282 return BinaryOperator::CreateNot(Builder.CreateSelect(A, One, B));
3283
3284 // DeMorgan in select form: !a || !b --> !(a && b)
3285 // select !a, true, !b --> not (select a, b, false)
3286 if (match(&SI, m_LogicalOr(m_Not(m_Value(A)), m_Not(m_Value(B)))) &&
3287 (CondVal->hasOneUse() || FalseVal->hasOneUse()) &&
3288 !match(A, m_ConstantExpr()) && !match(B, m_ConstantExpr()))
3289 return BinaryOperator::CreateNot(Builder.CreateSelect(A, B, Zero));
3290
3291 // select (select a, true, b), true, b -> select a, true, b
3292 if (match(CondVal, m_Select(m_Value(A), m_One(), m_Value(B))) &&
3293 match(TrueVal, m_One()) && match(FalseVal, m_Specific(B)))
3294 return replaceOperand(SI, 0, A);
3295 // select (select a, b, false), b, false -> select a, b, false
3296 if (match(CondVal, m_Select(m_Value(A), m_Value(B), m_Zero())) &&
3297 match(TrueVal, m_Specific(B)) && match(FalseVal, m_Zero()))
3298 return replaceOperand(SI, 0, A);
3299
3300 // ~(A & B) & (A | B) --> A ^ B
3301 if (match(&SI, m_c_LogicalAnd(m_Not(m_LogicalAnd(m_Value(A), m_Value(B))),
3302 m_c_LogicalOr(m_Deferred(A), m_Deferred(B)))))
3303 return BinaryOperator::CreateXor(A, B);
3304
3305 // select (~a | c), a, b -> select a, (select c, true, b), false
3306 if (match(CondVal,
3307 m_OneUse(m_c_Or(m_Not(m_Specific(TrueVal)), m_Value(C))))) {
3308 Value *OrV = Builder.CreateSelect(C, One, FalseVal);
3309 return SelectInst::Create(TrueVal, OrV, Zero);
3310 }
3311 // select (c & b), a, b -> select b, (select ~c, true, a), false
3312 if (match(CondVal, m_OneUse(m_c_And(m_Value(C), m_Specific(FalseVal))))) {
3313 if (Value *NotC = getFreelyInverted(C, C->hasOneUse(), &Builder)) {
3314 Value *OrV = Builder.CreateSelect(NotC, One, TrueVal);
3315 return SelectInst::Create(FalseVal, OrV, Zero);
3316 }
3317 }
3318 // select (a | c), a, b -> select a, true, (select ~c, b, false)
3319 if (match(CondVal, m_OneUse(m_c_Or(m_Specific(TrueVal), m_Value(C))))) {
3320 if (Value *NotC = getFreelyInverted(C, C->hasOneUse(), &Builder)) {
3321 Value *AndV = Builder.CreateSelect(NotC, FalseVal, Zero);
3322 return SelectInst::Create(TrueVal, One, AndV);
3323 }
3324 }
3325 // select (c & ~b), a, b -> select b, true, (select c, a, false)
3326 if (match(CondVal,
3327 m_OneUse(m_c_And(m_Value(C), m_Not(m_Specific(FalseVal)))))) {
3328 Value *AndV = Builder.CreateSelect(C, TrueVal, Zero);
3329 return SelectInst::Create(FalseVal, One, AndV);
3330 }
3331
3332 if (match(FalseVal, m_Zero()) || match(TrueVal, m_One())) {
3333 Use *Y = nullptr;
3334 bool IsAnd = match(FalseVal, m_Zero()) ? true : false;
3335 Value *Op1 = IsAnd ? TrueVal : FalseVal;
3336 if (isCheckForZeroAndMulWithOverflow(CondVal, Op1, IsAnd, Y)) {
3337 auto *FI = new FreezeInst(*Y, (*Y)->getName() + ".fr");
3338 InsertNewInstBefore(FI, cast<Instruction>(Y->getUser())->getIterator());
3339 replaceUse(*Y, FI);
3340 return replaceInstUsesWith(SI, Op1);
3341 }
3342
3343 if (auto *V = foldBooleanAndOr(CondVal, Op1, SI, IsAnd,
3344 /*IsLogical=*/true))
3345 return replaceInstUsesWith(SI, V);
3346 }
3347
3348 // select (a || b), c, false -> select a, c, false
3349 // select c, (a || b), false -> select c, a, false
3350 // if c implies that b is false.
3351 if (match(CondVal, m_LogicalOr(m_Value(A), m_Value(B))) &&
3352 match(FalseVal, m_Zero())) {
3353 std::optional<bool> Res = isImpliedCondition(TrueVal, B, DL);
3354 if (Res && *Res == false)
3355 return replaceOperand(SI, 0, A);
3356 }
3357 if (match(TrueVal, m_LogicalOr(m_Value(A), m_Value(B))) &&
3358 match(FalseVal, m_Zero())) {
3359 std::optional<bool> Res = isImpliedCondition(CondVal, B, DL);
3360 if (Res && *Res == false)
3361 return replaceOperand(SI, 1, A);
3362 }
3363 // select c, true, (a && b) -> select c, true, a
3364 // select (a && b), true, c -> select a, true, c
3365 // if c = false implies that b = true
3366 if (match(TrueVal, m_One()) &&
3367 match(FalseVal, m_LogicalAnd(m_Value(A), m_Value(B)))) {
3368 std::optional<bool> Res = isImpliedCondition(CondVal, B, DL, false);
3369 if (Res && *Res == true)
3370 return replaceOperand(SI, 2, A);
3371 }
3372 if (match(CondVal, m_LogicalAnd(m_Value(A), m_Value(B))) &&
3373 match(TrueVal, m_One())) {
3374 std::optional<bool> Res = isImpliedCondition(FalseVal, B, DL, false);
3375 if (Res && *Res == true)
3376 return replaceOperand(SI, 0, A);
3377 }
3378
3379 if (match(TrueVal, m_One())) {
3380 Value *C;
3381
3382 // (C && A) || (!C && B) --> sel C, A, B
3383 // (A && C) || (!C && B) --> sel C, A, B
3384 // (C && A) || (B && !C) --> sel C, A, B
3385 // (A && C) || (B && !C) --> sel C, A, B (may require freeze)
3386 if (match(FalseVal, m_c_LogicalAnd(m_Not(m_Value(C)), m_Value(B))) &&
3387 match(CondVal, m_c_LogicalAnd(m_Specific(C), m_Value(A)))) {
3388 auto *SelCond = dyn_cast<SelectInst>(CondVal);
3389 auto *SelFVal = dyn_cast<SelectInst>(FalseVal);
3390 bool MayNeedFreeze = SelCond && SelFVal &&
3391 match(SelFVal->getTrueValue(),
3392 m_Not(m_Specific(SelCond->getTrueValue())));
3393 if (MayNeedFreeze)
3394 C = Builder.CreateFreeze(C);
3395 return SelectInst::Create(C, A, B);
3396 }
3397
3398 // (!C && A) || (C && B) --> sel C, B, A
3399 // (A && !C) || (C && B) --> sel C, B, A
3400 // (!C && A) || (B && C) --> sel C, B, A
3401 // (A && !C) || (B && C) --> sel C, B, A (may require freeze)
3402 if (match(CondVal, m_c_LogicalAnd(m_Not(m_Value(C)), m_Value(A))) &&
3403 match(FalseVal, m_c_LogicalAnd(m_Specific(C), m_Value(B)))) {
3404 auto *SelCond = dyn_cast<SelectInst>(CondVal);
3405 auto *SelFVal = dyn_cast<SelectInst>(FalseVal);
3406 bool MayNeedFreeze = SelCond && SelFVal &&
3407 match(SelCond->getTrueValue(),
3408 m_Not(m_Specific(SelFVal->getTrueValue())));
3409 if (MayNeedFreeze)
3410 C = Builder.CreateFreeze(C);
3411 return SelectInst::Create(C, B, A);
3412 }
3413 }
3414
3415 return nullptr;
3416}
3417
3418// Return true if we can safely remove the select instruction for std::bit_ceil
3419// pattern.
3420static bool isSafeToRemoveBitCeilSelect(ICmpInst::Predicate Pred, Value *Cond0,
3421 const APInt *Cond1, Value *CtlzOp,
3422 unsigned BitWidth,
3423 bool &ShouldDropNUW) {
3424 // The challenge in recognizing std::bit_ceil(X) is that the operand is used
3425 // for the CTLZ proper and select condition, each possibly with some
3426 // operation like add and sub.
3427 //
3428 // Our aim is to make sure that -ctlz & (BitWidth - 1) == 0 even when the
3429 // select instruction would select 1, which allows us to get rid of the select
3430 // instruction.
3431 //
3432 // To see if we can do so, we do some symbolic execution with ConstantRange.
3433 // Specifically, we compute the range of values that Cond0 could take when
3434 // Cond == false. Then we successively transform the range until we obtain
3435 // the range of values that CtlzOp could take.
3436 //
3437 // Conceptually, we follow the def-use chain backward from Cond0 while
3438 // transforming the range for Cond0 until we meet the common ancestor of Cond0
3439 // and CtlzOp. Then we follow the def-use chain forward until we obtain the
3440 // range for CtlzOp. That said, we only follow at most one ancestor from
3441 // Cond0. Likewise, we only follow at most one ancestor from CtrlOp.
3442
3443 ConstantRange CR = ConstantRange::makeExactICmpRegion(
3444 CmpInst::getInversePredicate(Pred), *Cond1);
3445
3446 ShouldDropNUW = false;
3447
3448 // Match the operation that's used to compute CtlzOp from CommonAncestor. If
3449 // CtlzOp == CommonAncestor, return true as no operation is needed. If a
3450 // match is found, execute the operation on CR, update CR, and return true.
3451 // Otherwise, return false.
3452 auto MatchForward = [&](Value *CommonAncestor) {
3453 const APInt *C = nullptr;
3454 if (CtlzOp == CommonAncestor)
3455 return true;
3456 if (match(CtlzOp, m_Add(m_Specific(CommonAncestor), m_APInt(C)))) {
3457 CR = CR.add(*C);
3458 return true;
3459 }
3460 if (match(CtlzOp, m_Sub(m_APInt(C), m_Specific(CommonAncestor)))) {
3461 ShouldDropNUW = true;
3462 CR = ConstantRange(*C).sub(CR);
3463 return true;
3464 }
3465 if (match(CtlzOp, m_Not(m_Specific(CommonAncestor)))) {
3466 CR = CR.binaryNot();
3467 return true;
3468 }
3469 return false;
3470 };
3471
3472 const APInt *C = nullptr;
3473 Value *CommonAncestor;
3474 if (MatchForward(Cond0)) {
3475 // Cond0 is either CtlzOp or CtlzOp's parent. CR has been updated.
3476 } else if (match(Cond0, m_Add(m_Value(CommonAncestor), m_APInt(C)))) {
3477 CR = CR.sub(*C);
3478 if (!MatchForward(CommonAncestor))
3479 return false;
3480 // Cond0's parent is either CtlzOp or CtlzOp's parent. CR has been updated.
3481 } else {
3482 return false;
3483 }
3484
3485 // Return true if all the values in the range are either 0 or negative (if
3486 // treated as signed). We do so by evaluating:
3487 //
3488 // CR - 1 u>= (1 << BitWidth) - 1.
3489 APInt IntMax = APInt::getSignMask(BitWidth) - 1;
3490 CR = CR.sub(APInt(BitWidth, 1));
3491 return CR.icmp(ICmpInst::ICMP_UGE, IntMax);
3492}
3493
3494// Transform the std::bit_ceil(X) pattern like:
3495//
3496// %dec = add i32 %x, -1
3497// %ctlz = tail call i32 @llvm.ctlz.i32(i32 %dec, i1 false)
3498// %sub = sub i32 32, %ctlz
3499// %shl = shl i32 1, %sub
3500// %ugt = icmp ugt i32 %x, 1
3501// %sel = select i1 %ugt, i32 %shl, i32 1
3502//
3503// into:
3504//
3505// %dec = add i32 %x, -1
3506// %ctlz = tail call i32 @llvm.ctlz.i32(i32 %dec, i1 false)
3507// %neg = sub i32 0, %ctlz
3508// %masked = and i32 %ctlz, 31
3509// %shl = shl i32 1, %sub
3510//
3511// Note that the select is optimized away while the shift count is masked with
3512// 31. We handle some variations of the input operand like std::bit_ceil(X +
3513// 1).
3514static Instruction *foldBitCeil(SelectInst &SI, IRBuilderBase &Builder,
3515 InstCombinerImpl &IC) {
3516 Type *SelType = SI.getType();
3517 unsigned BitWidth = SelType->getScalarSizeInBits();
3518
3519 Value *FalseVal = SI.getFalseValue();
3520 Value *TrueVal = SI.getTrueValue();
3521 CmpPredicate Pred;
3522 const APInt *Cond1;
3523 Value *Cond0, *Ctlz, *CtlzOp;
3524 if (!match(SI.getCondition(), m_ICmp(Pred, m_Value(Cond0), m_APInt(Cond1))))
3525 return nullptr;
3526
3527 if (match(TrueVal, m_One())) {
3528 std::swap(FalseVal, TrueVal);
3529 Pred = CmpInst::getInversePredicate(Pred);
3530 }
3531
3532 bool ShouldDropNUW;
3533
3534 if (!match(FalseVal, m_One()) ||
3535 !match(TrueVal,
3536 m_OneUse(m_Shl(m_One(), m_OneUse(m_Sub(m_SpecificInt(BitWidth),
3537 m_Value(Ctlz)))))) ||
3538 !match(Ctlz, m_Intrinsic<Intrinsic::ctlz>(m_Value(CtlzOp), m_Zero())) ||
3539 !isSafeToRemoveBitCeilSelect(Pred, Cond0, Cond1, CtlzOp, BitWidth,
3540 ShouldDropNUW))
3541 return nullptr;
3542
3543 if (ShouldDropNUW)
3544 cast<Instruction>(CtlzOp)->setHasNoUnsignedWrap(false);
3545
3546 // Build 1 << (-CTLZ & (BitWidth-1)). The negation likely corresponds to a
3547 // single hardware instruction as opposed to BitWidth - CTLZ, where BitWidth
3548 // is an integer constant. Masking with BitWidth-1 comes free on some
3549 // hardware as part of the shift instruction.
3550
3551 // Drop range attributes and re-infer them in the next iteration.
3552 cast<Instruction>(Ctlz)->dropPoisonGeneratingAnnotations();
3553 IC.addToWorklist(cast<Instruction>(Ctlz));
3554 Value *Neg = Builder.CreateNeg(Ctlz);
3555 Value *Masked =
3556 Builder.CreateAnd(Neg, ConstantInt::get(SelType, BitWidth - 1));
3557 return BinaryOperator::Create(Instruction::Shl, ConstantInt::get(SelType, 1),
3558 Masked);
3559}
3560
3561// This function tries to fold the following operations:
3562// (x < y) ? -1 : zext(x != y)
3563// (x < y) ? -1 : zext(x > y)
3564// (x > y) ? 1 : sext(x != y)
3565// (x > y) ? 1 : sext(x < y)
3566// Into ucmp/scmp(x, y), where signedness is determined by the signedness
3567// of the comparison in the original sequence.
3568Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::foldSelectToCmp(SelectInst &SI) {
3569 Value *TV = SI.getTrueValue();
3570 Value *FV = SI.getFalseValue();
3571
3572 CmpPredicate Pred;
3573 Value *LHS, *RHS;
3574 if (!match(SI.getCondition(), m_ICmp(Pred, m_Value(LHS), m_Value(RHS))))
3575 return nullptr;
3576
3577 if (!LHS->getType()->isIntOrIntVectorTy())
3578 return nullptr;
3579
3580 // Try to swap operands and the predicate. We need to be careful when doing
3581 // so because two of the patterns have opposite predicates, so use the
3582 // constant inside select to determine if swapping operands would be
3583 // beneficial to us.
3584 if ((ICmpInst::isGT(Pred) && match(TV, m_AllOnes())) ||
3585 (ICmpInst::isLT(Pred) && match(TV, m_One()))) {
3586 Pred = ICmpInst::getSwappedPredicate(Pred);
3587 std::swap(LHS, RHS);
3588 }
3589 bool IsSigned = ICmpInst::isSigned(Pred);
3590
3591 bool Replace = false;
3592 CmpPredicate ExtendedCmpPredicate;
3593 // (x < y) ? -1 : zext(x != y)
3594 // (x < y) ? -1 : zext(x > y)
3595 if (ICmpInst::isLT(Pred) && match(TV, m_AllOnes()) &&
3596 match(FV, m_ZExt(m_c_ICmp(ExtendedCmpPredicate, m_Specific(LHS),
3597 m_Specific(RHS)))) &&
3598 (ExtendedCmpPredicate == ICmpInst::ICMP_NE ||
3599 ICmpInst::getSwappedPredicate(ExtendedCmpPredicate) == Pred))
3600 Replace = true;
3601
3602 // (x > y) ? 1 : sext(x != y)
3603 // (x > y) ? 1 : sext(x < y)
3604 if (ICmpInst::isGT(Pred) && match(TV, m_One()) &&
3605 match(FV, m_SExt(m_c_ICmp(ExtendedCmpPredicate, m_Specific(LHS),
3606 m_Specific(RHS)))) &&
3607 (ExtendedCmpPredicate == ICmpInst::ICMP_NE ||
3608 ICmpInst::getSwappedPredicate(ExtendedCmpPredicate) == Pred))
3609 Replace = true;
3610
3611 // (x == y) ? 0 : (x > y ? 1 : -1)
3612 CmpPredicate FalseBranchSelectPredicate;
3613 const APInt *InnerTV, *InnerFV;
3614 if (Pred == ICmpInst::ICMP_EQ && match(TV, m_Zero()) &&
3615 match(FV, m_Select(m_c_ICmp(FalseBranchSelectPredicate, m_Specific(LHS),
3616 m_Specific(RHS)),
3617 m_APInt(InnerTV), m_APInt(InnerFV)))) {
3618 if (!ICmpInst::isGT(FalseBranchSelectPredicate)) {
3619 FalseBranchSelectPredicate =
3620 ICmpInst::getSwappedPredicate(FalseBranchSelectPredicate);
3621 std::swap(LHS, RHS);
3622 }
3623
3624 if (!InnerTV->isOne()) {
3625 std::swap(InnerTV, InnerFV);
3626 std::swap(LHS, RHS);
3627 }
3628
3629 if (ICmpInst::isGT(FalseBranchSelectPredicate) && InnerTV->isOne() &&
3630 InnerFV->isAllOnes()) {
3631 IsSigned = ICmpInst::isSigned(FalseBranchSelectPredicate);
3632 Replace = true;
3633 }
3634 }
3635
3636 Intrinsic::ID IID = IsSigned ? Intrinsic::scmp : Intrinsic::ucmp;
3637 if (Replace)
3638 return replaceInstUsesWith(
3639 SI, Builder.CreateIntrinsic(SI.getType(), IID, {LHS, RHS}));
3640 return nullptr;
3641}
3642
3643bool InstCombinerImpl::fmulByZeroIsZero(Value *MulVal, FastMathFlags FMF,
3644 const Instruction *CtxI) const {
3645 KnownFPClass Known = computeKnownFPClass(MulVal, FMF, fcNegative, CtxI);
3646
3647 return Known.isKnownNeverNaN() && Known.isKnownNeverInfinity() &&
3648 (FMF.noSignedZeros() || Known.signBitIsZeroOrNaN());
3649}
3650
3651static bool matchFMulByZeroIfResultEqZero(InstCombinerImpl &IC, Value *Cmp0,
3652 Value *Cmp1, Value *TrueVal,
3653 Value *FalseVal, Instruction &CtxI,
3654 bool SelectIsNSZ) {
3655 Value *MulRHS;
3656 if (match(Cmp1, m_PosZeroFP()) &&
3657 match(TrueVal, m_c_FMul(m_Specific(Cmp0), m_Value(MulRHS)))) {
3658 FastMathFlags FMF = cast<FPMathOperator>(TrueVal)->getFastMathFlags();
3659 // nsz must be on the select, it must be ignored on the multiply. We
3660 // need nnan and ninf on the multiply for the other value.
3661 FMF.setNoSignedZeros(SelectIsNSZ);
3662 return IC.fmulByZeroIsZero(MulRHS, FMF, &CtxI);
3663 }
3664
3665 return false;
3666}
3667
3668/// Check whether the KnownBits of a select arm may be affected by the
3669/// select condition.
3670static bool hasAffectedValue(Value *V, SmallPtrSetImpl<Value *> &Affected,
3671 unsigned Depth) {
3672 if (Depth == MaxAnalysisRecursionDepth)
3673 return false;
3674
3675 // Ignore the case where the select arm itself is affected. These cases
3676 // are handled more efficiently by other optimizations.
3677 if (Depth != 0 && Affected.contains(V))
3678 return true;
3679
3680 if (auto *I = dyn_cast<Instruction>(V)) {
3681 if (isa<PHINode>(I)) {
3682 if (Depth == MaxAnalysisRecursionDepth - 1)
3683 return false;
3684 Depth = MaxAnalysisRecursionDepth - 2;
3685 }
3686 return any_of(I->operands(), [&](Value *Op) {
3687 return Op->getType()->isIntOrIntVectorTy() &&
3688 hasAffectedValue(Op, Affected, Depth + 1);
3689 });
3690 }
3691
3692 return false;
3693}
3694
3695// This transformation enables the possibility of transforming fcmp + sel into
3696// a fmaxnum/fminnum intrinsic.
3697static Value *foldSelectIntoAddConstant(SelectInst &SI,
3698 InstCombiner::BuilderTy &Builder) {
3699 // Do this transformation only when select instruction gives NaN and NSZ
3700 // guarantee.
3701 auto *SIFOp = dyn_cast<FPMathOperator>(&SI);
3702 if (!SIFOp || !SIFOp->hasNoSignedZeros() || !SIFOp->hasNoNaNs())
3703 return nullptr;
3704
3705 auto TryFoldIntoAddConstant =
3706 [&Builder, &SI](CmpInst::Predicate Pred, Value *X, Value *Z,
3707 Instruction *FAdd, Constant *C, bool Swapped) -> Value * {
3708 // Only these relational predicates can be transformed into maxnum/minnum
3709 // intrinsic.
3710 if (!CmpInst::isRelational(Pred) || !match(Z, m_AnyZeroFP()))
3711 return nullptr;
3712
3713 if (!match(FAdd, m_FAdd(m_Specific(X), m_Specific(C))))
3714 return nullptr;
3715
3716 Value *NewSelect = Builder.CreateSelect(SI.getCondition(), Swapped ? Z : X,
3717 Swapped ? X : Z, "", &SI);
3718 NewSelect->takeName(&SI);
3719
3720 Value *NewFAdd = Builder.CreateFAdd(NewSelect, C);
3721 NewFAdd->takeName(FAdd);
3722
3723 // Propagate FastMath flags
3724 FastMathFlags SelectFMF = SI.getFastMathFlags();
3725 FastMathFlags FAddFMF = FAdd->getFastMathFlags();
3726 FastMathFlags NewFMF = FastMathFlags::intersectRewrite(SelectFMF, FAddFMF) |
3727 FastMathFlags::unionValue(SelectFMF, FAddFMF);
3728 cast<Instruction>(NewFAdd)->setFastMathFlags(NewFMF);
3729 cast<Instruction>(NewSelect)->setFastMathFlags(NewFMF);
3730
3731 return NewFAdd;
3732 };
3733
3734 // select((fcmp Pred, X, 0), (fadd X, C), C)
3735 // => fadd((select (fcmp Pred, X, 0), X, 0), C)
3736 //
3737 // Pred := OGT, OGE, OLT, OLE, UGT, UGE, ULT, and ULE
3738 Instruction *FAdd;
3739 Constant *C;
3740 Value *X, *Z;
3741 CmpPredicate Pred;
3742
3743 // Note: OneUse check for `Cmp` is necessary because it makes sure that other
3744 // InstCombine folds don't undo this transformation and cause an infinite
3745 // loop. Furthermore, it could also increase the operation count.
3746 if (match(&SI, m_Select(m_OneUse(m_FCmp(Pred, m_Value(X), m_Value(Z))),
3747 m_OneUse(m_Instruction(FAdd)), m_Constant(C))))
3748 return TryFoldIntoAddConstant(Pred, X, Z, FAdd, C, /*Swapped=*/false);
3749
3750 if (match(&SI, m_Select(m_OneUse(m_FCmp(Pred, m_Value(X), m_Value(Z))),
3751 m_Constant(C), m_OneUse(m_Instruction(FAdd)))))
3752 return TryFoldIntoAddConstant(Pred, X, Z, FAdd, C, /*Swapped=*/true);
3753
3754 return nullptr;
3755}
3756
3757Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::visitSelectInst(SelectInst &SI) {
3758 Value *CondVal = SI.getCondition();
3759 Value *TrueVal = SI.getTrueValue();
3760 Value *FalseVal = SI.getFalseValue();
3761 Type *SelType = SI.getType();
3762
3763 if (Value *V = simplifySelectInst(CondVal, TrueVal, FalseVal,
3764 SQ.getWithInstruction(&SI)))
3765 return replaceInstUsesWith(SI, V);
3766
3767 if (Instruction *I = canonicalizeSelectToShuffle(SI))
3768 return I;
3769
3770 if (Instruction *I = canonicalizeScalarSelectOfVecs(SI, *this))
3771 return I;
3772
3773 // If the type of select is not an integer type or if the condition and
3774 // the selection type are not both scalar nor both vector types, there is no
3775 // point in attempting to match these patterns.
3776 Type *CondType = CondVal->getType();
3777 if (!isa<Constant>(CondVal) && SelType->isIntOrIntVectorTy() &&
3778 CondType->isVectorTy() == SelType->isVectorTy()) {
3779 if (Value *S = simplifyWithOpReplaced(TrueVal, CondVal,
3780 ConstantInt::getTrue(CondType), SQ,
3781 /* AllowRefinement */ true))
3782 return replaceOperand(SI, 1, S);
3783
3784 if (Value *S = simplifyWithOpReplaced(FalseVal, CondVal,
3785 ConstantInt::getFalse(CondType), SQ,
3786 /* AllowRefinement */ true))
3787 return replaceOperand(SI, 2, S);
3788
3789 if (replaceInInstruction(TrueVal, CondVal,
3790 ConstantInt::getTrue(CondType)) ||
3791 replaceInInstruction(FalseVal, CondVal,
3792 ConstantInt::getFalse(CondType)))
3793 return &SI;
3794 }
3795
3796 if (Instruction *R = foldSelectOfBools(SI))
3797 return R;
3798
3799 // Selecting between two integer or vector splat integer constants?
3800 //
3801 // Note that we don't handle a scalar select of vectors:
3802 // select i1 %c, <2 x i8> <1, 1>, <2 x i8> <0, 0>
3803 // because that may need 3 instructions to splat the condition value:
3804 // extend, insertelement, shufflevector.
3805 //
3806 // Do not handle i1 TrueVal and FalseVal otherwise would result in
3807 // zext/sext i1 to i1.
3808 if (SelType->isIntOrIntVectorTy() && !SelType->isIntOrIntVectorTy(1) &&
3809 CondVal->getType()->isVectorTy() == SelType->isVectorTy()) {
3810 // select C, 1, 0 -> zext C to int
3811 if (match(TrueVal, m_One()) && match(FalseVal, m_Zero()))
3812 return new ZExtInst(CondVal, SelType);
3813
3814 // select C, -1, 0 -> sext C to int
3815 if (match(TrueVal, m_AllOnes()) && match(FalseVal, m_Zero()))
3816 return new SExtInst(CondVal, SelType);
3817
3818 // select C, 0, 1 -> zext !C to int
3819 if (match(TrueVal, m_Zero()) && match(FalseVal, m_One())) {
3820 Value *NotCond = Builder.CreateNot(CondVal, "not." + CondVal->getName());
3821 return new ZExtInst(NotCond, SelType);
3822 }
3823
3824 // select C, 0, -1 -> sext !C to int
3825 if (match(TrueVal, m_Zero()) && match(FalseVal, m_AllOnes())) {
3826 Value *NotCond = Builder.CreateNot(CondVal, "not." + CondVal->getName());
3827 return new SExtInst(NotCond, SelType);
3828 }
3829 }
3830
3831 auto *SIFPOp = dyn_cast<FPMathOperator>(&SI);
3832
3833 if (auto *FCmp = dyn_cast<FCmpInst>(CondVal)) {
3834 FCmpInst::Predicate Pred = FCmp->getPredicate();
3835 Value *Cmp0 = FCmp->getOperand(0), *Cmp1 = FCmp->getOperand(1);
3836 // Are we selecting a value based on a comparison of the two values?
3837 if ((Cmp0 == TrueVal && Cmp1 == FalseVal) ||
3838 (Cmp0 == FalseVal && Cmp1 == TrueVal)) {
3839 // Canonicalize to use ordered comparisons by swapping the select
3840 // operands.
3841 //
3842 // e.g.
3843 // (X ugt Y) ? X : Y -> (X ole Y) ? Y : X
3844 if (FCmp->hasOneUse() && FCmpInst::isUnordered(Pred)) {
3845 FCmpInst::Predicate InvPred = FCmp->getInversePredicate();
3846 // FIXME: The FMF should propagate from the select, not the fcmp.
3847 Value *NewCond = Builder.CreateFCmpFMF(InvPred, Cmp0, Cmp1, FCmp,
3848 FCmp->getName() + ".inv");
3849 Value *NewSel =
3850 Builder.CreateSelectFMF(NewCond, FalseVal, TrueVal, FCmp);
3851 return replaceInstUsesWith(SI, NewSel);
3852 }
3853 }
3854
3855 if (SIFPOp) {
3856 // Fold out scale-if-equals-zero pattern.
3857 //
3858 // This pattern appears in code with denormal range checks after it's
3859 // assumed denormals are treated as zero. This drops a canonicalization.
3860
3861 // TODO: Could relax the signed zero logic. We just need to know the sign
3862 // of the result matches (fmul x, y has the same sign as x).
3863 //
3864 // TODO: Handle always-canonicalizing variant that selects some value or 1
3865 // scaling factor in the fmul visitor.
3866
3867 // TODO: Handle ldexp too
3868
3869 Value *MatchCmp0 = nullptr;
3870 Value *MatchCmp1 = nullptr;
3871
3872 // (select (fcmp [ou]eq x, 0.0), (fmul x, K), x => x
3873 // (select (fcmp [ou]ne x, 0.0), x, (fmul x, K) => x
3874 if (Pred == CmpInst::FCMP_OEQ || Pred == CmpInst::FCMP_UEQ) {
3875 MatchCmp0 = FalseVal;
3876 MatchCmp1 = TrueVal;
3877 } else if (Pred == CmpInst::FCMP_ONE || Pred == CmpInst::FCMP_UNE) {
3878 MatchCmp0 = TrueVal;
3879 MatchCmp1 = FalseVal;
3880 }
3881
3882 if (Cmp0 == MatchCmp0 &&
3883 matchFMulByZeroIfResultEqZero(*this, Cmp0, Cmp1, MatchCmp1, MatchCmp0,
3884 SI, SIFPOp->hasNoSignedZeros()))
3885 return replaceInstUsesWith(SI, Cmp0);
3886 }
3887 }
3888
3889 if (SIFPOp) {
3890 // TODO: Try to forward-propagate FMF from select arms to the select.
3891
3892 auto *FCmp = dyn_cast<FCmpInst>(CondVal);
3893
3894 // Canonicalize select of FP values where NaN and -0.0 are not valid as
3895 // minnum/maxnum intrinsics.
3896 if (SIFPOp->hasNoNaNs() && SIFPOp->hasNoSignedZeros()) {
3897 Value *X, *Y;
3898 if (match(&SI, m_OrdOrUnordFMax(m_Value(X), m_Value(Y)))) {
3899 Value *BinIntr =
3900 Builder.CreateBinaryIntrinsic(Intrinsic::maxnum, X, Y, &SI);
3901 if (auto *BinIntrInst = dyn_cast<Instruction>(BinIntr))
3902 BinIntrInst->setHasNoNaNs(FCmp->hasNoNaNs());
3903 return replaceInstUsesWith(SI, BinIntr);
3904 }
3905
3906 if (match(&SI, m_OrdOrUnordFMin(m_Value(X), m_Value(Y)))) {
3907 Value *BinIntr =
3908 Builder.CreateBinaryIntrinsic(Intrinsic::minnum, X, Y, &SI);
3909 if (auto *BinIntrInst = dyn_cast<Instruction>(BinIntr))
3910 BinIntrInst->setHasNoNaNs(FCmp->hasNoNaNs());
3911 return replaceInstUsesWith(SI, BinIntr);
3912 }
3913 }
3914 }
3915
3916 // Fold selecting to fabs.
3917 if (Instruction *Fabs = foldSelectWithFCmpToFabs(SI, *this))
3918 return Fabs;
3919
3920 // See if we are selecting two values based on a comparison of the two values.
3921 if (CmpInst *CI = dyn_cast<CmpInst>(CondVal))
3922 if (Instruction *NewSel = foldSelectValueEquivalence(SI, *CI))
3923 return NewSel;
3924
3925 if (ICmpInst *ICI = dyn_cast<ICmpInst>(CondVal))
3926 if (Instruction *Result = foldSelectInstWithICmp(SI, ICI))
3927 return Result;
3928
3929 if (Instruction *Add = foldAddSubSelect(SI, Builder))
3930 return Add;
3931 if (Instruction *Add = foldOverflowingAddSubSelect(SI, Builder))
3932 return Add;
3933 if (Instruction *Or = foldSetClearBits(SI, Builder))
3934 return Or;
3935 if (Instruction *Mul = foldSelectZeroOrMul(SI, *this))
3936 return Mul;
3937
3938 // Turn (select C, (op X, Y), (op X, Z)) -> (op X, (select C, Y, Z))
3939 auto *TI = dyn_cast<Instruction>(TrueVal);
3940 auto *FI = dyn_cast<Instruction>(FalseVal);
3941 if (TI && FI && TI->getOpcode() == FI->getOpcode())
3942 if (Instruction *IV = foldSelectOpOp(SI, TI, FI))
3943 return IV;
3944
3945 if (Instruction *I = foldSelectExtConst(SI))
3946 return I;
3947
3948 if (Instruction *I = foldSelectWithSRem(SI, *this, Builder))
3949 return I;
3950
3951 // Fold (select C, (gep Ptr, Idx), Ptr) -> (gep Ptr, (select C, Idx, 0))
3952 // Fold (select C, Ptr, (gep Ptr, Idx)) -> (gep Ptr, (select C, 0, Idx))
3953 auto SelectGepWithBase = [&](GetElementPtrInst *Gep, Value *Base,
3954 bool Swap) -> GetElementPtrInst * {
3955 Value *Ptr = Gep->getPointerOperand();
3956 if (Gep->getNumOperands() != 2 || Gep->getPointerOperand() != Base ||
3957 !Gep->hasOneUse())
3958 return nullptr;
3959 Value *Idx = Gep->getOperand(1);
3960 if (isa<VectorType>(CondVal->getType()) && !isa<VectorType>(Idx->getType()))
3961 return nullptr;
3962 Type *ElementType = Gep->getSourceElementType();
3963 Value *NewT = Idx;
3964 Value *NewF = Constant::getNullValue(Idx->getType());
3965 if (Swap)
3966 std::swap(NewT, NewF);
3967 Value *NewSI =
3968 Builder.CreateSelect(CondVal, NewT, NewF, SI.getName() + ".idx", &SI);
3969 return GetElementPtrInst::Create(ElementType, Ptr, NewSI,
3970 Gep->getNoWrapFlags());
3971 };
3972 if (auto *TrueGep = dyn_cast<GetElementPtrInst>(TrueVal))
3973 if (auto *NewGep = SelectGepWithBase(TrueGep, FalseVal, false))
3974 return NewGep;
3975 if (auto *FalseGep = dyn_cast<GetElementPtrInst>(FalseVal))
3976 if (auto *NewGep = SelectGepWithBase(FalseGep, TrueVal, true))
3977 return NewGep;
3978
3979 // See if we can fold the select into one of our operands.
3980 if (SelType->isIntOrIntVectorTy() || SelType->isFPOrFPVectorTy()) {
3981 if (Instruction *FoldI = foldSelectIntoOp(SI, TrueVal, FalseVal))
3982 return FoldI;
3983
3984 Value *LHS, *RHS;
3985 Instruction::CastOps CastOp;
3986 SelectPatternResult SPR = matchSelectPattern(&SI, LHS, RHS, &CastOp);
3987 auto SPF = SPR.Flavor;
3988 if (SPF) {
3989 Value *LHS2, *RHS2;
3990 if (SelectPatternFlavor SPF2 = matchSelectPattern(LHS, LHS2, RHS2).Flavor)
3991 if (Instruction *R = foldSPFofSPF(cast<Instruction>(LHS), SPF2, LHS2,
3992 RHS2, SI, SPF, RHS))
3993 return R;
3994 if (SelectPatternFlavor SPF2 = matchSelectPattern(RHS, LHS2, RHS2).Flavor)
3995 if (Instruction *R = foldSPFofSPF(cast<Instruction>(RHS), SPF2, LHS2,
3996 RHS2, SI, SPF, LHS))
3997 return R;
3998 }
3999
4000 if (SelectPatternResult::isMinOrMax(SPF)) {
4001 // Canonicalize so that
4002 // - type casts are outside select patterns.
4003 // - float clamp is transformed to min/max pattern
4004
4005 bool IsCastNeeded = LHS->getType() != SelType;
4006 Value *CmpLHS = cast<CmpInst>(CondVal)->getOperand(0);
4007 Value *CmpRHS = cast<CmpInst>(CondVal)->getOperand(1);
4008 if (IsCastNeeded ||
4009 (LHS->getType()->isFPOrFPVectorTy() &&
4010 ((CmpLHS != LHS && CmpLHS != RHS) ||
4011 (CmpRHS != LHS && CmpRHS != RHS)))) {
4012 CmpInst::Predicate MinMaxPred = getMinMaxPred(SPF, SPR.Ordered);
4013
4014 Value *Cmp;
4015 if (CmpInst::isIntPredicate(MinMaxPred))
4016 Cmp = Builder.CreateICmp(MinMaxPred, LHS, RHS);
4017 else
4018 Cmp = Builder.CreateFCmpFMF(MinMaxPred, LHS, RHS,
4019 cast<Instruction>(SI.getCondition()));
4020
4021 Value *NewSI = Builder.CreateSelect(Cmp, LHS, RHS, SI.getName(), &SI);
4022 if (!IsCastNeeded)
4023 return replaceInstUsesWith(SI, NewSI);
4024
4025 Value *NewCast = Builder.CreateCast(CastOp, NewSI, SelType);
4026 return replaceInstUsesWith(SI, NewCast);
4027 }
4028 }
4029 }
4030
4031 // See if we can fold the select into a phi node if the condition is a select.
4032 if (auto *PN = dyn_cast<PHINode>(SI.getCondition()))
4033 if (Instruction *NV = foldOpIntoPhi(SI, PN))
4034 return NV;
4035
4036 if (SelectInst *TrueSI = dyn_cast<SelectInst>(TrueVal)) {
4037 if (TrueSI->getCondition()->getType() == CondVal->getType()) {
4038 // Fold nested selects if the inner condition can be implied by the outer
4039 // condition.
4040 if (Value *V = simplifyNestedSelectsUsingImpliedCond(
4041 *TrueSI, CondVal, /*CondIsTrue=*/true, DL))
4042 return replaceOperand(SI, 1, V);
4043
4044 // select(C0, select(C1, a, b), b) -> select(C0&C1, a, b)
4045 // We choose this as normal form to enable folding on the And and
4046 // shortening paths for the values (this helps getUnderlyingObjects() for
4047 // example).
4048 if (TrueSI->getFalseValue() == FalseVal && TrueSI->hasOneUse()) {
4049 Value *And = Builder.CreateLogicalAnd(CondVal, TrueSI->getCondition());
4050 replaceOperand(SI, 0, And);
4051 replaceOperand(SI, 1, TrueSI->getTrueValue());
4052 return &SI;
4053 }
4054 }
4055 }
4056 if (SelectInst *FalseSI = dyn_cast<SelectInst>(FalseVal)) {
4057 if (FalseSI->getCondition()->getType() == CondVal->getType()) {
4058 // Fold nested selects if the inner condition can be implied by the outer
4059 // condition.
4060 if (Value *V = simplifyNestedSelectsUsingImpliedCond(
4061 *FalseSI, CondVal, /*CondIsTrue=*/false, DL))
4062 return replaceOperand(SI, 2, V);
4063
4064 // select(C0, a, select(C1, a, b)) -> select(C0|C1, a, b)
4065 if (FalseSI->getTrueValue() == TrueVal && FalseSI->hasOneUse()) {
4066 Value *Or = Builder.CreateLogicalOr(CondVal, FalseSI->getCondition());
4067 replaceOperand(SI, 0, Or);
4068 replaceOperand(SI, 2, FalseSI->getFalseValue());
4069 return &SI;
4070 }
4071 }
4072 }
4073
4074 // Try to simplify a binop sandwiched between 2 selects with the same
4075 // condition. This is not valid for div/rem because the select might be
4076 // preventing a division-by-zero.
4077 // TODO: A div/rem restriction is conservative; use something like
4078 // isSafeToSpeculativelyExecute().
4079 // select(C, binop(select(C, X, Y), W), Z) -> select(C, binop(X, W), Z)
4080 BinaryOperator *TrueBO;
4081 if (match(TrueVal, m_OneUse(m_BinOp(TrueBO))) && !TrueBO->isIntDivRem()) {
4082 if (auto *TrueBOSI = dyn_cast<SelectInst>(TrueBO->getOperand(0))) {
4083 if (TrueBOSI->getCondition() == CondVal) {
4084 replaceOperand(*TrueBO, 0, TrueBOSI->getTrueValue());
4085 Worklist.push(TrueBO);
4086 return &SI;
4087 }
4088 }
4089 if (auto *TrueBOSI = dyn_cast<SelectInst>(TrueBO->getOperand(1))) {
4090 if (TrueBOSI->getCondition() == CondVal) {
4091 replaceOperand(*TrueBO, 1, TrueBOSI->getTrueValue());
4092 Worklist.push(TrueBO);
4093 return &SI;
4094 }
4095 }
4096 }
4097
4098 // select(C, Z, binop(select(C, X, Y), W)) -> select(C, Z, binop(Y, W))
4099 BinaryOperator *FalseBO;
4100 if (match(FalseVal, m_OneUse(m_BinOp(FalseBO))) && !FalseBO->isIntDivRem()) {
4101 if (auto *FalseBOSI = dyn_cast<SelectInst>(FalseBO->getOperand(0))) {
4102 if (FalseBOSI->getCondition() == CondVal) {
4103 replaceOperand(*FalseBO, 0, FalseBOSI->getFalseValue());
4104 Worklist.push(FalseBO);
4105 return &SI;
4106 }
4107 }
4108 if (auto *FalseBOSI = dyn_cast<SelectInst>(FalseBO->getOperand(1))) {
